blocks:
Sūrat al-Naḥl (The Bee)

Meccan, and it has 128 verses.

Sūrat al-Naḥl (The Bee)

Forty verses from its beginning are Meccan, and the remainder, starting from His saying: (وَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا فِي اللهِ) (and those who migrated for the sake of Allah) until the end of the Sūrah, are Medinan. And it is said that the entire Sūrah is Meccan, except for three verses: (وَإِنْ عاقَبْتُمْ) (And if you punish) until the end of the Sūrah. The number of its verses is one hundred and twenty-eight verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) أَتى أَمْرُ اللهِ فَلا تَسْتَعْجِلُوهُ (1) Allah’s command has come, so do not seek to hasten it. It is said that they were seeking to hasten what the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had promised them: the establishment of the Hour and Allah’s destruction of them, just as He did on the Day of Badr, doing so out of mockery and denial. They would say: "If what you say is true, then the idols will intercede for us and save us from it." So, this [verse] was revealed. The meaning is that the promised command is equivalent to that which is certainly coming and realized, in that its occurrence is inevitable. Therefore, do not seek to hasten its occurrence, for there is no good for you in it, nor any escape from it. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed when Quraysh asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to send down the punishment upon them, so Allah revealed: أَتى أَمْرُ اللهِ فَلا تَسْتَعْجِلُوهُ. (Allah’s command has come, so do not seek to hasten it.) And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When Allah informs that something will be, it is as if it has already been. سُبْحانَهُ(١) وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (1) Immaculate is He and exalted above what they associate [with Him]. He is absolved and majestic, far above having a partner who could repel what He intends for them. And it was recited with the tāʾ (تَعَالَتْ).

footnote (١) This is a word of declaring Allah Almighty immaculate (*تنزيه*) from what is unfitting for Him and His attributes, and declaring Him immaculate from having a partner in His worship; meaning, He is majestic (*جلّ*), sanctified (*تقدّس*), and declared immaculate (*تنزّه*) from having a partner. He is exalted (*تعالى*), magnified (*تعظم*), and elevated above all attributes of deficiency.

(٢) يُنَزِّلُ الْمَلائِكَةَ بِالرُّوحِ ** (He sends down the angels with the Spirit) by which He revives the hearts deadened by ignorance, namely the revelation (الوحي) and the Quran. Al-Qummi says it means the power (القّوة) which Allah placed in them. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He says: By the Book (الكتاب) and Prophethood (النبوّة). And it was recited as يُنَزِّلُ (He sends down) from أنزل (He sent down), and تُنَزَّلُ (it is sent down) in the passive voice (المبنيّ للمفعول) and with the doubling (التشديد).

مِنْ أَمْرِهِ ** (by His command) From His dominion (ملكوته).

عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ. (to whomever of His servants He wishes.)

In al-Basair, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: (It refers to) Gabriel (جبرئيل) who descended upon the Prophets, and the Spirit (الروح) is with them and with the Successors (الأوصياء). It does not leave them; it grants them understanding (يفقّههم) and guides them rightly (يسدّدهم) from Allah. The narration. And another discussion concerning the Spirit (الروح) will come in Surat Bani Isra'il, if Allah wills, and its complete investigation has already preceded in Surat al-Hijr.

أَنْ أَنْذِرُوا ** (saying, 'Warn [the people]) By informing (them), derived from the phrase 'I warned him of such-and-such' (أنذرت بكذا) when I informed him.

أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاتَّقُونِ. (that there is no god except Me; so be wary of Me.')

(٣) خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ تَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ. (3) He created the heavens and the earth with the truth. Exalted is He above [what they associate with Him]! (٤) خَلَقَ الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ فَإِذا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُبِينٌ. (4) He created man from a drop of semen, and behold, he is an open contender. القمّيّ said: He created him from a drop of foul water, and then he becomes a contender (خصيماً), a speaker (متكلّماً), eloquent (بليغاً). (٥) وَالْأَنْعامَ ** (5) And the cattle— The eight pairs خَلَقَها لَكُمْ فِيها دِفْءٌ. He created them for you. In them is warmth, القمّيّ That by which they warm themselves, which is taken from their wool (صوفها) and their fur (وبرها). وَمَنافِعُ ** and benefits, Their offspring (نسلها), their milk (درّها), their backs (ظهورها), the plowing of the earth (واثارة الأرض), and what is compensated by them (وما يعوّض بها). وَمِنْها تَأْكُلُونَ ** and of them you eat. Meaning: you eat what is edible from them, such as meats (اللّحُوم), fats (الشحُوم), and milks (الألبان).

(٦) وَلَكُمْ فِيها جَمالٌ زينة حِينَ تُرِيحُونَ تردّونها من مراعيها إلى مراحها بالعشّي وَحِينَ تَسْرَحُونَ تخرجونها بالغداة إلى المرعى (6) And there is beauty for you in them when you bring them home and when you drive them out [to pasture].

meaning: adornment (زينة). when you bring them home meaning: you return them from their pastures (مراعيها) to their resting place (مراحها) in the evening (بالعشّي). and when you drive them out [to pasture]. meaning: you take them out in the morning (بالغداة) to the pasture (المرعى). For indeed, the courtyards (الأفنية) are adorned by them during the two times, and their owners are esteemed in the eyes of those who look upon them. And the returning (الاراحة) is mentioned first because the beauty in it is more manifest, for they approach with full bellies and full udders. Then they retreat to the enclosures (الحظاير), being present for their owners.

(٧) وَتَحْمِلُ أَثْقالَكُمْ ** (7) and carry your loads Your burdens (أحمالكم). إِلى بَلَدٍ لَمْ تَكُونُوا بالِغِيهِ ** to a land which you could not reach if you were not able to reach it, let alone carrying them on your backs to it. إِلَّا بِشِقِّ الْأَنْفُسِ ** except with great hardship. except with difficulty (كلفة) and hardship (مشقّة). إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ ** Indeed your Lord is most kind, all-merciful. where He had mercy upon you by creating them for your benefit and for the ease of the matter upon you.

(٨) وَالْخَيْلَ وَالْبِغالَ وَالْحَمِيرَ لِتَرْكَبُوها وَزِينَةً وَيَخْلُقُ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ. (8) and horses, mules and asses, for you to ride on, and as an adornment. And He creates what you do not know. al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the wonders (العجائب) which Allah created on land and in the sea.

(٩) وَعَلَى اللهِ قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ ** (9) With Allah rests the guidance to the straight path, and some paths are deviant. Had He wished, He would have guided you all. [This means] the guidance (هداية) to the straight path (الطريق المستقيم) which leads to the Truth (الحق), and similar to it is [the saying]: إِنَّ عَلَيْنا لَلْهُدى (Indeed, guidance is incumbent upon Us). وَمِنْها جائِرٌ [This means] deviating (حائد) from the straight path (القصد). وَلَوْ شاءَ لَهَداكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** [He would have guided you] to the straight path (القصد).

(١٠) هُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً لَكُمْ مِنْهُ شَرابٌ وَمِنْهُ شَجَرٌ ** (10) It is He who sends down water from the sky, from which you have a drink, and from which come the trees wherein you pasture [your cattle]. And from it (منه) comes vegetation (نبات). فِيهِ تُسِيمُونَ You graze (ترعون) your livestock (مواشيكم) therein.

(١١) يُنْبِتُ لَكُمْ بِهِ الزَّرْعَ وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالنَّخِيلَ وَالْأَعْنابَ وَمِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ ** (11) With it He grows for you crops, olives, date-palms, grapes, and all kinds of fruits. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who reflect. So they deduce from it the greatness (عظمة) of its Creator (خالقها), the perfection (كمال) of His power (قدرته), and His wisdom (حكمته). And it was recited (وقرئ) نُنْبِتُ with the nūn (بالنّون).

(١٢) وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ وَالنُّجُومُ ** (And He has subjected to you the night and the day, and the sun and the moon, and the stars) By preparing them for your benefits, being subjected by His command. And it was recited with the rafʿ (رفع) (nominative case) of النجوم (the stars) and مسخرات (subjected), and also the rafʿ of الشمس (the sun) and القمر (the moon). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ ** (There are indeed signs in that for a people who apply reason) He mentioned the signs (الآيات) in the plural here and mentioned reason (العقل) instead of thought (الفكر), because in the celestial effects (الآثار العلويّة) there are types of indications (الدلالة) that are manifest to those who apply reason (للعقلاءِ) regarding the greatness of Allah.

(١٣) **وَما ذَرَأَ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (13) and whatever He has multiplied for you in the earth

And He subjected to you what He created for you in the earth, of animal, and plant, and mineral, مُخْتَلِفاً أَلْوانُهُ (different are its colors/kinds)—meaning: its types (أصنافه), for they usually differ by color—إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ. (There is indeed a sign in that for a people who take admonition.)

(14) وَهُوَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ الْبَحْرَ ** (14) And He is the One who has subjugated the sea He subjugated it such that you are enabled to benefit from it through sailing, fishing, and diving. لِتَأْكُلُوا مِنْهُ لَحْماً طَرِيًّا ** that you may eat fresh meat from it which is the fish. وَتَسْتَخْرِجُوا مِنْهُ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَها (١) and extract from it ornaments that you wear such as pearls and coral. وَتَرَى الْفُلْكَ ** and you see the ships (meaning) the vessels مَواخِرَ فِيهِ ** ploughing through it sailing within it, cleaving it with their chests (بِحَيَازِيمِهَا), derived from al-makhr (المخر), which is the splitting of the water. And it is said (it means) the sound of the ships' movement. وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** and that you may seek of His bounty (meaning) from the abundance of His provision by sailing them for trade. وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ and so that you may give thanks. Meaning: you recognize the favor (نعمة) of Allah, and thus fulfill its due right.

footnote (١) That is, they adorn themselves with them and you make your women wear them. Were it not for His subjugation (*تَسْخِيرُهُ*) of that, glory be to Him, you would not have been able to approach it or dive into it. (*م ن*)

(١٥) وَأَلْقى فِي الْأَرْضِ رَواسِيَ (And He cast firm mountains in the earth) fixed mountains (جبالاً ثوابت), أَنْ تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ (lest it should shake with you) out of dislike (كراهة) that it should incline (تميل) with you and shake (وتضطربَ).

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them all), that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, when Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created the seas, they boasted and surged, saying: 'What thing can overcome me?' So Allah created the Ark (الفلك) and made it revolve upon them and subdued them. Then the earth boasted and said: 'What thing can overcome me?' So Allah created the mountains and fixed them upon its back as pegs (أوتاداً), preventing it from shaking with what is upon it. Thus, the earth became submissive (ذلت) and settled (واستقرت)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah made the Imams (الأئمّة) the pillars (أركان) of the earth, lest it should shake with its inhabitants."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "If the Imam (الإِمام) were removed from the earth for an hour, it would shake with its inhabitants just as the sea shakes with its inhabitants."

وَأَنْهاراً وَسُبُلاً لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (and rivers and ways, so that you may be guided) وَأَنْهاراً ** (and rivers) means and He placed in it rivers (أَنْهاراً) and ways (وَسُبُلاً), لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (so that you may be guided) to your destinations (مقاصدكم).

(16) وَعَلاماتٍ ** (And landmarks). These are the signs (معالم) of the roads and everything by which travelers find guidance, such as mountains, plains, and other things. وَبِالنَّجْمِ هُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ ** (And by the stars they are guided) at night in the deserts and the seas.

In al-Kafi, al-Majma', al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, in many narrations from them (peace be upon them): "We are the landmarks (العلامات), and the star (النَّجْمِ) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: بِالنَّجْمِ هُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ ** (And by the stars they are guided) refers to al-Jady (الجدي) because it is a star that does not move, and upon it is the structure (بناء) of the Qiblah, and by it the people of the land and the sea are guided.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "The apparent (ظاهر) and the inner (باطن) meaning is al-Jady (الجدي); the Qiblah is structured upon it, and by it the people of the land and the sea are guided because it does not move."

I say: Meaning, its apparent (الظاهر) meaning is al-Jady (الجدي), and its inner (الباطن) meaning is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١٧) أَفَمَنْ يَخْلُقُ كَمَنْ لا يَخْلُقُ ** (17) Is He who creates like him who does not create? meaning the idols (الأصنام). أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ ** Will you not then take admonition? so that you may know the corruption (فساد) of that. (١٨) وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا (١) نِعْمَةَ اللهِ لا تُحْصُوها ** (18) If you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you cannot count them. You cannot ascertain their number, let alone be able to fulfill the performance of their gratitude (شكرها). إِنَّ اللهَ لَغَفُورٌ ** Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, since He overlooks your shortcoming (تقصيركم) in performing its gratitude (شكرها). رَحِيمٌ ** all-merciful. He does not cut them off due to your negligence (تفريطكم) concerning it, nor does He hasten the punishment (العقوبة) upon you for your ingratitude (كفرانها) towards them.

(١٩) وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما تُسِرُّونَ وَما تُعْلِنُونَ ** (19) And Allah knows what you conceal and what you disclose. concerning your beliefs (عقائدكم) and your deeds (أعمالكم), and this is a threat (وعيد). (٢٠) وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (20) And those whom they invoke besides Allah (meaning) the deities (الآلهة) whom you worship besides Him. And it was recited (تَدْعُونَ) with the tāʾ (بالتاءِ). لا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْئاً وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ. do not create anything, and they are [themselves] created. (٢١) أَمْواتٌ (٢) غَيْرُ أَحْياءٍ وَما يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ ** (21) They are dead, not living, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected. They do not know the time of their resurrection (بعثهم) or the resurrection of their worshippers (عبدتهم). So how can there be a time for them to receive recompense (جزاءٍ) for their worship? (٢٢) إِلهُكُمْ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ فَالَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ مُنْكِرَةٌ وَهُمْ مُسْتَكْبِرُونَ. (22) Your God is the One God. But those who do not believe in the Hereafter—their hearts are in denial, and they are arrogant. (٢٣) لا جَرَمَ ** Truly (حقّاً) أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما يُسِرُّونَ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ ** certainly Allah knows what they conceal and what they disclose. So He will recompense them, and this is a threat (وعيد). إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْتَكْبِرِينَ.** Indeed, He does not love the arrogant.

Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ ** (do not believe in the Hereafter) means mercy (الرحمة). قُلُوبُهُمْ مُنْكِرَةٌ ** (their hearts are in denial) means disbelieving (كافرة). وَهُمْ مُسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** (and they are arrogant) means regarding the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْتَكْبِرِينَ (Indeed, He does not love the arrogant) means regarding the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him).

And al-Ayyashi (narrated): Al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both) passed by some poor people (مساكين) who had spread out a cloak (كساءً) and thrown down pieces of bread (كِسَراً). They said: "Come, O son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم!" So, he bent his side (وركه) and ate with them. Then He recited: Indeed, Allah لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْتَكْبِرِينَ. (does not love the arrogant.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever goes about thinking that he has superiority (فضلاً) over another is among the arrogant (المستكبرين)." It was said: "He only sees that he has superiority over him due to well-being (بالعافية) when he sees him committing acts of disobedience (المعاصي)." He (peace be upon him) said: "Far from it! Far from it! Perhaps what he has committed has been forgiven for him, while you are detained and held to account. Have you not recited the story of the sorcerers of Moses (peace be upon him)?"

footnote (١) Its meaning is that if you wished to count the blessings (*نعم*) of Allah, the Glorified, upon you and know their details, you would not be able to enumerate them or count them. Rather, you can only know their general summary. M.N.

footnote (٢) He confirmed their state of being dead by His saying **غَيْرُ أَحْياءٍ ** (not living) in order to negate life from them absolutely. For among the dead (*الأموات*) are those who previously had a state of life and have an awaited state of life, contrary to the idols (*الأصنام*), which have neither a previous nor an awaited life. He said *أموات* (dead, plural of one who was alive) and did not say *موات* (inanimate/lifeless matter), even though *الأموات* is the plural of *الميّت* (the dead one) who had life which then ceased, because they fashioned the idols (*الأَصنام*) in the images and forms of rational beings (*العقلاء*) and treated them as rational beings, both in naming and in belief. For this reason, He said **لا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْئاً وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ ** (do not create anything, and they are [themselves] created). *Majmaʿ al-Bayān*.

(٢٤) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ ما ذا أَنْزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ قالُوا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (24) When they are told, 'What has your Lord sent down?' they say, 'Myths of the ancients.' The narratives of the ancients and their falsehoods (أباطيل). (٢٥) لِيَحْمِلُوا أَوْزارَهُمْ كامِلَةً يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَمِنْ أَوْزارِ الَّذِينَ يُضِلُّونَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ أَلا ساءَ ما يَزِرُونَ ** (25) That they may bear their burdens completely on the Day of Resurrection, and some of the burdens of those whom they mislead without knowledge. Look! Evil is what they bear! That is, they said that in order to mislead people and turn them away (صَدّاً) from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). Thus, they bore the burdens (أوزار) of their misguidance completely, and some of the burdens (أوزار) of those whom they misled, because the one who is misguided (الضّالّ) and the one who misleads (المضلّ) are partners. This one misleads him, and that one obeys him in his misguidance without knowledge (بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ). This means they mislead those who do not know that they are misguided (ضلّال). The ignorant person (الجاهل) is not excused only because it is incumbent upon him to search and look with his intellect until he distinguishes between the truthful (المحق) and the false (المبطِل).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding ما ذا أَنْزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ ** (What has your Lord sent down?), [it was revealed] concerning Ali (peace be upon him). قالُوا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (they say, 'Myths of the ancients.') They encouraged the people of Jahiliyyah (Ignorance) in their Jahiliyyah لِيَحْمِلُوا أَوْزارَهُمْ ** (That they may bear their burdens) so that they might complete the disbelief (الكفر) for the Day of Resurrection. وَمِنْ أَوْزارِ الَّذِينَ يُضِلُّونَهُمْ ** (and some of the burdens of those whom they mislead) means the disbelief (كفر) of those who follow them (يَتَوَلَّوْنَهُمْ).

And in al-Qummi: They bear their sins (آثامهم), meaning those who usurped the right of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and the sins (آثام) of everyone who followed their example. This is the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, no cupping-glass (محجمة) of blood was spilled, nor was a staff struck against a staff, nor was an unlawful private part (فرج حرام) seized, nor was wealth taken unlawfully (من غير حله), except that the burden (وزر) of that is upon their necks, without diminishing anything from the burdens (أوزار) of the perpetrators."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): Whichever caller (داعٍ) calls to guidance (الهدى) and is followed, he shall have the like of their rewards (أجورهم) without diminishing anything from their rewards. And whichever caller (داعٍ) calls to misguidance (ضلالة) and is followed in it, then upon him shall be the like of the burdens (أوزار) of those who followed him, without diminishing anything from their burdens (أوزارهم).

(26) قَدْ مَكَرَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتَى اللهُ بُنْيانَهُمْ مِنَ الْقَواعِدِ ** (26) Those who were before them certainly plotted, but Allah came upon their structure from the foundations. [Meaning] from the pillars (الأساطين) upon which they built فَخَرَّ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّقْفُ مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ ** and the roof fell on them from above them. This is a metaphor for their utter destruction due to their plotting. The meaning is that they established structures (منصوبات) by which they sought to plot against Allah, but Allah made their destruction reside within those very structures, like the situation of a people who built a structure and supported it with pillars (الأساطين). Then the structure was approached from the direction of the pillars (الأساطين)—in that they were weakened (ضعضعت)—so the roof fell upon them and they perished. And among their parables is the one who dug a pit (جبّاً) for his brother, only to fall into it headlong (منكبّاً). What is meant by Allah's coming (إتيان الله) is the coming of His command (إتيان أمره) from the foundations (القواعد), meaning, from the direction of the foundations (جهة القواعد). **وَأَتاهُمُ الْعَذابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** and the punishment overtook them from where they did not perceive. They did not reckon [it] nor did they expect [it].

In al-Jawami' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited [the verse as]: "but Allah came upon their house (بيتهم)." And al-Ayyashi added: meaning, the house of their plotting (بيت مكرهم).

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was a house of treachery (بيت غدر) where they would gather when they intended evil (الشرّ).

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): [It means] the house of their plotting (بيت مكرهم), meaning they died, and Allah cast them into the Fire. He said: And this is a parable for the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: So His coming upon their structure from the foundations (إتيانه بنيانهم من القواعد) is the sending of the punishment (إرسال العذاب).

(٢٧) ثُمَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ يُخْزِيهِمْ ** He will humiliate them وَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنْتُمْ تُشَاقُّونَ فِيهِمْ ** you used to show enmity towards the believers and dispute with them concerning their matter. And it was recited with a kasrah on the nūn (تُشَاقُّونِّي), meaning: you used to defy Me, because defying the believers is defying Allah. قالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ** meaning the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the scholars (العلماء) who used to call them to Monotheism (التوحيد), but they would defy them and act arrogantly towards them. إِنَّ الْخِزْيَ الْيَوْمَ وَالسُّوءَ ** the humiliation (الذلة) and the punishment (العذاب) عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ as an expression of gloating (الشّماتة) and an increase in humiliation (الاهانة).

(27) Then on the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them, and say, 'Where are My partners, for whose sake you used to defy [the faithful]?' Those who were given knowledge will say, 'Indeed, today disgrace and misery are upon the faithless.'

Al-Qummi said: الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ** (Those who were given knowledge) are the Imams (peace be upon them). They will say to their enemies: "Where are your partners and those whom you obeyed in the world (الدنيا)?"

(٢٨) الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (28) those whom the angels take away meaning, the angels of punishment, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-Nisa' concerning the parallel of this verse. And it was recited with the yā' (يَتَوَفَّاهُمْ), [referring to] those who wronged their souls (ظَالِمِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ) by exposing them to the eternal punishment. فَأَلْقَوُا السَّلَمَ ** then they offered submission, so they submitted (سَالَمُوا) and humbled themselves (أَخْبَتُوا) when they witnessed death. ما كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ مِنْ سُوءٍ ** [saying,] 'We were not doing any evil.' They denied what was found from them of disbelief (الكُفْر) and transgression (العُدْوَان) in the world (الدُّنْيَا). بَلى ** 'Yes, indeed! Those possessing knowledge (أُولُوا العِلْم) refuted them. إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** Allah is indeed aware of what you used to do.' So He will recompense you for it. And this, too, is part of the gloating (الشَّمَاتَة), and likewise [the following verse].

(٢٩) فَادْخُلُوا أَبْوابَ جَهَنَّمَ ** (29) Enter the gates of hell, Every group (صنف) [will enter] its gate (بابها) prepared for it (المعدَّ لهُ). خالِدِينَ فِيها فَلَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ ** to abide therein. Evil is the abode of the arrogant. [This abode is] Hell (جهنم). (٣٠) وَقِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا ما ذا أَنْزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ قالُوا خَيْراً ** (30) And when it is said to the Godwary, 'What has your Lord sent down?' they say, 'Good.' They made the answer conform to the question, acknowledging the sending down (الإِنزال), unlike the deniers (الجاحدين) when قالُوا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** they said, 'Fables of the ancients,' and it is not part of the sending down (الإِنزال) in any way. لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هذِهِ الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةٌ For those who do good in this world there is a good reward (حَسَنَةٌ), [meaning] a recompense (مكافأة) in this world. وَلَدارُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ and the abode of the Hereafter is better. Meaning, and their reward (ثوابهم) in the Hereafter is better than it [the worldly reward]. And this is a promise (عدة) for those who are Godwary (اتّقوا). And it is permissible that what follows it, which is the completion of their speech, is a substitute (بدلاً) and an explanation (تفسيراً) for 'good' (خير). وَلَنِعْمَ دارُ الْمُتَّقِينَ And excellent is the abode of the Godwary.

(31) جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ (Gardens of Eden) Residence and eternity. يَدْخُلُونَها تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ لَهُمْ فِيها ما يَشاؤُنَ (which they will enter, with rivers running in them, where they will have whatever they wish) —of the various kinds of desired things (المشتهيات). And narratives (أخبار) concerning the matter of the Gardens of Eden have already passed in Surat al-Tawbah. كَذلِكَ يَجْزِي اللهُ الْمُتَّقِينَ. (Thus does Allah reward the Godwary.)

In al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Hold fast to the fear of Allah (تقوى الله), for it gathers all goodness, and there is no goodness besides it. Through it, one attains goodness—both the goodness of this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخِرة)—that cannot be attained through anything else." The Almighty, the Majestic, said: وَقِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا (And it will be said to those who were Godwary)—and He recited this verse.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): وَلَنِعْمَ دارُ الْمُتَّقِينَ (And surely excellent is the abode of the Godwary)—[He said:] the world (الدنيا).

(٣٢) الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (Those whom the angels take away) meaning the angels of mercy, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-Nisa' (Chapter of Women). طَيِّبِينَ ** (while they are good) due to the angels giving them glad tidings of Paradise. **يَقُولُونَ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (saying, 'Peace be to you!) safety for you from every evil. Enter Paradise because of what you used to do.

Al-Qummi, regarding His saying: طَيِّبِينَ ** (while they are good), said: They are the believers whose births (مواليدهم) were pure.

And in al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): No one among mankind departs his body until his soul knows to which of the two abodes it will proceed: to Paradise or the Fire, whether he is an enemy of Allah or a friend (وليّ). If he is a friend (وليّ) of Allah, the gates of Paradise are opened for him, and its paths are laid out for him, and he looks at what Allah has prepared for him therein, so he is relieved of every concern and every burden is lifted from him. But if he is an enemy of Allah, the gates of the Fire are opened for him, and its paths are laid out for him, and he looks at what Allah has prepared for him therein, so he faces every detestable thing and every evil descends upon him. All of this occurs at the time of death, and at that time, it occurs with certainty. The Almighty Allah said: الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ظالِمِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ. (Those whom the angels take away while they are wronging themselves.) the Ayah.

(٣٣) هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ ** Is it that they await Do those who do not believe in the Hereafter await إِلَّا أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ ** the angels of punishment (ملائكة العذاب) to seize their souls (أرواحِهِم). And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء). أَوْ يَأْتِيَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ. or the command of your Lord should come. Al-Qummi (said it refers to) the punishment (العذاب), death (الموت), and the emergence of the Riser (peace be upon him). كَذلِكَ like that action of polytheism (الشرك) and denial (التكذيب) فَعَلَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ وَما ظَلَمَهُمُ اللهُ by destroying them وَلكِنْ كانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ but they used to wrong themselves through their disbelief (كفرهم) and their acts of disobedience (معاصيهم) leading to it.

(٣٤) فَأَصابَهُمْ سَيِّئاتُ ما عَمِلُوا وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (So the evil consequences of what they had done afflicted them, and they were besieged by what they used to ridicule.) And its recompense encompassed them. And al-hayq (الحيق) is not used except for evil (الشر). Al-Qummi (said regarding) ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (what they used to ridicule): (It is) the punishment in al-Raj'ah (الرجعة).

وَقالَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا لَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ما عَبَدْنا مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ نَحْنُ وَلا آباؤُنا وَلا حَرَّمْنا مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ كَذلِكَ فَعَلَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ (٣٥) (35) The polytheists say, 'Had Allah wished, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him, neither we nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything without [a sanction from] Him.' Those who were before them did the same.

They associated partners with Allah, and they forbade what Allah had made lawful, and they committed what Allah had made unlawful. So when they were alerted to the ugliness of their deeds, they attributed them to Allah and said: لَوْ شاءَ اللهُ (Had Allah wished) we would not have done them. فَهَلْ عَلَى الرُّسُلِ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ (Is the apostles' duty only to communicate in a clear manner?) except the conveyance that clarifies the truth.

(٣٦) وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنا فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولاً أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَاجْتَنِبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ هَدَى اللهُ ** (Certainly We raised an apostle in every nation, [saying:] 'Worship Allah, and shun the Tāghūt.' Then among them were some whom Allah guided,) He granted them success (توفيق) for faith because they were among the people of Divine Favor (اللطف). وَمِنْهُمْ (١) مَنْ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِ الضَّلالَةُ ** (and among them were some who deserved to be in error.) since He abandoned them (خذلهم) and did not grant them success (توفيق) due to their determination upon disbelief (الكفر). فَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ. (So travel in the land,)

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Allah never sent a prophet except with our Wilayah (ولايتنا) and dissociation (البراءة) from our enemies." And that is His saying, the Almighty: وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنا ** (Certainly We raised) the Ayah up to His saying مَنْ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِ الضَّلالَةُ (some who deserved to be in error), meaning, due to their denial (تكذيبهم).

And al-Qummi (meaning: in the accounts of those who perished before him): فَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (and observe how was the fate of the deniers.) such as 'Ad and Thamud and others, so that perhaps you may take heed (تعتبرون).

footnote (١) And among them are those who turned away from what the Messenger called them to, so Allah abandoned him (*خذله*), and the error (*الضّلالة*) became fixed upon him and clung to him, so he will never believe. And it was said that its meaning is that the error (*الضّلالة*) became incumbent upon him, which is the punishment (*العذاب*) and destruction (*الهلاك*). And it was said that its meaning is: And among them are those upon whom the penalty (*عقوبة*) of error (*الضّلالة*) became incumbent. And Allah, the Glorified, has named the penalty (*العقاب*) error (*ضلالاً*) in His saying: إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ فِي ضَلالٍ وَسُعُرٍ (Indeed the guilty are in error and a blaze). *Majma' al-Bayan*.

(٣٧) إِنْ تَحْرِصْ ** O Muhammad, عَلى هُداهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي مَنْ يُضِلُ ** meaning, those whom He forsakes (يَخْذُلُهُ). وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ meaning, those who help them. إِنْ تَحْرِصْ عَلى هُداهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي مَنْ يُضِلُ وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ (Even if you are eager for their guidance, Allah does not guide those whom He leads astray, and they will have no helpers.)

(٣٨) وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ لا يَبْعَثُ اللهُ مَنْ يَمُوتُ ** It was said that this refers to those who associated partners [with Allah] (الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا); just as they denied Monotheism (التَّوْحِيد), they denied the Resurrection, swearing oaths concerning it. بَلى ** He will certainly raise them. وَعْداً عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** that they will be resurrected, either due to their lack of knowledge that it is among the necessities of wisdom (مَوَاجِبِ الْحِكْمَة), or due to the deficiency of their perception regarding what is customary (بِالْمَأْلُوف), so they imagine its impossibility. وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ لا يَبْعَثُ اللهُ مَنْ يَمُوتُ بَلى وَعْداً عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ** (They have sworn by Allah with their most solemn oaths that Allah will not raise up those who die. Yes, it is a promise binding upon Him in truth, but most people do not know.)

(٣٩) لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ meaning, He will resurrect them لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ ** (39) that He may clarify for them that about which they differ, which is the truth, وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ كانُوا كاذِبِينَ (and that the faithless may know that they were liars) in what they used to claim. (٤٠) إِنَّما قَوْلُنا لِشَيْءٍ إِذا أَرَدْناهُ أَنْ نَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** (40) All that We say to a thing, when We will it, is that We say to it ‘Be,’ and it is. And it was recited with the fath on the nūn (فَيَكُونَ) as a clarification of the possibility of the Resurrection (البعث). This is what the exegetes (المفسّرون) have said in the interpretation of these verses.

In al-Kāfī and al-Ayyāshī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him), that He said to Abū Basīr: "What do you say about this verse?" He replied: "The polytheists (المشركين) claim and swear to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that Allah will not resurrect the dead." He (peace be upon him) said: "Woe to whoever says this! Ask them: Did the polytheists swear by Allah or by al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā?" He said: I said: "May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), then show me its true meaning." He (peace be upon him) said to me: "O Abū Basīr, when our Qā’im (قائمنا) rises, Allah will resurrect a group of our followers (شيعتنا) with their swords sheathed on their shoulders. This will reach a group of our followers who have not died, and they will say: 'So-and-so, so-and-so, and so-and-so have been resurrected from their graves, and they are with the Qā’im.' This will reach a group of our enemies, and they will say: 'O company of the Shīʿah (يا معشر الشيعة), how deceitful you are! This is your state (دولتكم), and you speak lies concerning it. Nay, by Allah, these people have not lived, nor will they live until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).' He said: Then Allah recounted their saying and said: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ لا يَبْعَثُ اللهُ مَنْ يَمُوتُ. (And they swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths that Allah will not raise up those who die.)

And in al-Qummī, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), that He said: "What do the people say about this verse?" It was said: "They say it was revealed concerning the disbelievers (الكفّار)." He said: "The disbelievers do not swear (by Allah). Rather, it was revealed concerning a group from the Ummah (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم who were told that they would return after death before the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and they swear that they will not return. So Allah refuted them and said: لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ كانُوا كاذِبِينَ ** (that He may clarify for them that about which they differ, and that the faithless may know that they were liars), meaning concerning the Return (الرّجعة). He will return them, then kill them, and heal the breasts of the believers concerning them."

And in al-Ayyāshī, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), that He said: "What do the people say about this verse?" It was said: "They say there is no Resurrection (قيامة), no raising (بعث), and no gathering (نشور)." He said: "They have lied, by Allah! That is only when the Qā’im (القائم) rises and those who are returned (المكرّون) return with him. Then the people who oppose you will say: 'Your state (دولتكم) has appeared, O company of the Shīʿah (يا معشر الشيعة), and this is part of your lies! You claim that so-and-so and so-and-so have returned. Nay, by Allah, Allah will not resurrect those who die.' Do you not see that He said: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ ** (And they swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths)? The polytheists (المشركون) held al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā in greater reverence than to swear by anything else. So Allah said: بَلى وَعْداً عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ** (Yes, it is a promise binding upon Him in truth, that He may clarify for them that about which they differ) [referring to] the three verses.

(٤١) وَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا فِي اللهِ ** (41) And those who migrated for the sake of Allah for His sake and for His countenance (وجهه) مِنْ بَعْدِ ما ظُلِمُوا ** after they had been wronged, It was said that they are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the emigrants (المهاجرون) whom Quraysh wronged, so some of them emigrated (هاجر) to al-Habashah (الحَبَشة) and then to al-Madinah (المدينة), and those imprisoned and tortured (المحبوسون المعذّبون) in Mecca after the emigration (هجرة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions. لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةً We will surely settle them in a good place in the world, a good settlement (مبأة حسنة), which is the dominance (الغلبة) over the people of Mecca who wronged them, and over the Arabs entirely (قاطبةً), and over the people of the East and the West. وَلَأَجْرُ الْآخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ** and the reward of the Hereafter is surely greater, than what is hastened for them in the world. لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ (١)** had they known. (41)

(٤٢) الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا (42) those who are patient, regarding the harm inflicted by the disbelievers and the separation from their homeland, وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ and in their Lord put their trust. entrusting all matters entirely to Him. (٤٣) وَما أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجالاً نُوحِي إِلَيْهِمْ (43) We did not send before you, except men to whom We revealed (the message). This is a refutation of their saying that Allah is too great to send a human being like us to us. The explanation of the wisdom therein has already preceded in Surat al-An'am, narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Perhaps a reference to something similar is made by His saying: فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ So ask the People of the Reminder, meaning, the aspect of wisdom therein. فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ. So ask the People of the Reminder, if you do not know. In al-Kafi, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from them (peace be upon them) in numerous reports: The Messenger of Allah is the Reminder (الذِّكْر), and His Household (أهل بيتِه) are those who are questioned, and they are أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ (the People of the Reminder). And it is added in al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Allah the Almighty said: قَدْ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْراً رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ اللهِ Allah has certainly sent down to you a Reminder, a Messenger who recites to you the signs of Allah. So, the Reminder (الذِّكْر) is the Messenger of Allah, and we are His people (أهله). In al-Basair, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Reminder (الذِّكْر) is the Quran, and its people (أهله) are the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). And it is added in al-Kafi: Allah commanded that they be questioned, and they were not commanded to question the ignorant. And Allah named the Quran the Reminder (ذِكْراً), saying: وَأَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الذِّكْرَ لِتُبَيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ ما نُزِّلَ إِلَيْهِمْ. And We have sent down the Reminder to you, that you may clarify for the people what has been sent down to them. And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, those among us claim that Allah's saying: فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ (So ask the People of the Reminder) refers to the Jews and the Christians." He said: "Then they would call you to their religion." Then He struck His hand upon His chest and said: "We are the People of the Reminder (أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ), and we are those who are questioned." In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), is the like of it. And al-Ayyashi added: He said: "The Reminder (الذِّكْر) is the Quran." In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "There are obligations upon the Imams that are not upon their followers (شيعتهم), and there are obligations upon our followers that are not upon us. Allah commanded them to question us." He said: فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ So ask the People of the Reminder, if you do not know. "Thus, He commanded them to question us, but the answer is not obligatory upon us; if we wish, we answer, and if we wish, we withhold." And the like of this is narrated from Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Rida (peace be upon them both). I say: What is derived from these reports is that those addressed by the command to question are the believers, not the polytheists (المشركين), and that what is questioned about is everything that is difficult for them, rather than merely the fact that the messengers were men. This is only sound if وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا (We did not send) is not a refutation directed at the polytheists, or if فَسْئَلُوا (So ask) is a new, independent statement, or if the verse is one whose arrangement has been altered. This is especially true if His saying: بِالْبَيِّناتِ وَالزُّبُرِ with manifest proofs and scriptures is connected to His saying: أَرْسَلْنا We sent, since this statement (the command to ask) lies between them. As for commanding the polytheists to ask the Household (أهل البيت) about the messengers being men and not angels, despite their lack of faith in Allah and His Messenger, there is no justification for it, unless they are asked to explain the wisdom therein, and there is (difficulty) in that.

footnote (1) Meaning, if the disbelievers knew that. And it is said that its meaning is: If the believers knew the details of what Allah has prepared for them in Paradise, they would increase in joy and eagerness to adhere firmly to the religion. (م ن).

(٤٤) بِالْبَيِّناتِ وَالزُّبُرِ ** (with manifest proofs and scriptures.) It was said: meaning, We sent them with the miracles (المعجزات) and the Books (الكتب). As if it is the answer to one who asks: "With what were they sent?" وَأَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الذِّكْرَ ** (And We have sent down to you the Reminder) meaning the Quran, as preceded just now. It was named ذِكْرًا (Reminder) because it is an admonition (موعظة) and a warning (تنبيه). لِتُبَيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ ما نُزِّلَ إِلَيْهِمْ ** (so that you may clarify for the people what has been sent down to them) from what they were commanded and what they were forbidden. وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (and so that they may reflect.) and the desire that they reflect upon it, so that they become aware of the realities (الحقائق) and the gnoses (المعارف).

(٤٥) أَفَأَمِنَ الَّذِينَ مَكَرُوا السَّيِّئاتِ أَنْ يَخْسِفَ اللهُ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضَ ** (45) Do those who devise evil schemes feel secure that Allah will not make the earth swallow them? just as He made the earth swallow Qārūn, أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ الْعَذابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** or that the punishment will not overtake them from where they are unaware? suddenly, as He did to the people of Lūṭ. (٤٦) أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ فِي تَقَلُّبِهِمْ ** (46) Or that He will not seize them in their going to and fro? when they come and go in their trades and their works, for they are not able to frustrate Him. (٤٧) أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ عَلى تَخَوُّفٍ ** (47) Or that He will not seize them gradually? meaning, upon fear (على مخافة), by Him destroying a people before them, so they become fearful, and then the punishment comes to them while they are fearful; or, upon gradual reduction (على تنقص), by Him reducing them little by little in their souls and their wealth until they are destroyed, derived from (تخوّفته) when you reduce it (تنقّصته).

Al-Qummi said: [It means] upon vigilance (على تيقظ). And generally, this is contrary to His saying: مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَشْعُرُونَ. (from where they are unaware.)

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the enemies of Allah, and they will be transformed (يمسخون), cast down (يقذفون), and wander (يسيحون) in the earth.

And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sajjād (peace be upon him) in a discourse of His concerning admonition and asceticism (الزهد) regarding the world: "And do not be among the heedless (الغافلين), inclined toward the splendor (زهرة) of the world, those who devise evil schemes. For Allah says in His decisive Book: أَفَأَمِنَ الَّذِينَ مَكَرُوا السَّيِّئاتِ أَنْ يَخْسِفَ اللهُ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضَ (Do those who devise evil schemes feel secure that Allah will not make the earth swallow them?)—the Ayah. So beware of what Allah has warned you about through what He did to the wrongdoers (الظلمة) in His Book, and do not feel secure that some of what He promised the oppressive people (القوم الظالمين) in the Book will descend upon you. By Allah, Allah has admonished you in His Book through others, for the fortunate one (السعيد) is he who is admonished by others."

**فَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (47) Indeed your Lord is all-kind, all-merciful. since He does not hasten the punishment upon them.

(٤٨) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلى ما خَلَقَ اللهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** (48) Have they not regarded what Allah has created of things? The interrogation is one of denial (استفهام إنكار), meaning: They have indeed seen examples of these creations (الصنايع), so what is wrong with them that they do not reflect upon them, so that the perfection of His power and His subjugation (قهره) may become manifest to them, leading them to fear Him? And it was recited: أَوَلَمْ تَرَوْا (Have you not regarded) with the tāʾ (بالتّاءِ). يَتَفَيَّؤُا ظِلالُهُ ** whose shadows incline Meaning: Have they not looked at the creatures which have inclining shadows (ظلال متفيّئة)? And it was recited: تَتَفَيَّؤُ with the tāʾ (بالتاءِ). عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَالشَّمائِلِ ** to the right and the left, Meaning: From our right sides (أيماننا) and our left sides (شمائلنا). The singular form of some [words] and the plural form of others [in the verse] is based on consideration of the wording (اللفظ) and the meaning (المعنى). سُجَّداً لِلَّهِ وَهُمْ داخِرُونَ ** prostrating to Allah, while they are abased. [They are] submitting to Him (مستسلمين له), obedient (منقادين), and they are humbled (صاغرون) due to the actions of Allah within them.

Al-Qummi said: The shifting of every shadow that Allah has created is prostration (سجود) to Allah. It was said: It is also possible that the intent of His saying: وَهُمْ داخِرُونَ (while they are abased) is that the bodies (الأجرام) themselves are also abased (داخرة), humbled (صاغرة), and obedient (منقادة) to Allah, the Glorified, in what He does within them. They were only pluralized using the wāw and the nūn (بالواو والنّون) because abasement (الدخور) is among the descriptions of those possessing intellect (العقلاءِ).

(٤٩) وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (49) And to Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth submits (ينقاد) مِنْ دابَّةٍ (of creatures). This is a clarification (بيان) for both of them (i.e., what is in the heavens and what is in the earth), because al-dabbib (الدّبيب) is the bodily movement (الحركة الجسمانية), whether it is on earth or in heaven. And وَالْمَلائِكَةُ (the angels) are among those who have no location (لا مكان له).

And al-Qummi said regarding الْمَلائِكَةُ (the angels): [They are] what Allah has decreed for them, you pass through it (تمرون فيه). وَهُمْ لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (and they do not show arrogance) from His worship.

(٥٠) يَخافُونَ رَبَّهُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ ** (50) They fear their Lord above them, They fear Him, while He is above them by virtue of subjugation (القهر). وَهُوَ الْقاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبادِهِ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ.* and He is the All-dominant over His servants, and they do what they are commanded.

In al-Majma': It has been authentically narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that Allah has angels in the seventh heavens, prostrating from the time He created them until the Day of Resurrection. Their flanks tremble from the fear of Allah. Not a single drop falls from their tears except that it becomes an angel. When the Day of Resurrection comes, they will raise their heads and say: "We have not worshipped You with the worship that is rightfully due to You."

Some people of gnosis (أهل المعرفة) said that examples of these verses indicate that the entire universe is in the station (مقام) of witnessing (الشهود) and worship (العبادة), except for every creature that possesses the power of contemplation (التفكر). And these are none other than the articulate souls (النفوس الناطقة), specifically the human and animal souls, in terms of the essence (أعيان) of their selves, not in terms of their physical structures (هياكلهم). For their physical structures (هياكلهم) are like the rest of the universe in glorifying Him (التسبيح) and prostrating (السجود). Thus, all the limbs of the body are glorifying (مسبّحة) and articulate (ناطقة). Do you not see them bearing witness against the souls subjugated to them on the Day of Resurrection—from the skins, the hands, the legs, the tongues, the hearing, the sight, and all the faculties (القوى)? So, the judgment belongs to Allah, the Most High, the Great (العليّ الكبير). Further clarification for this station (المقام) will come in Sūrat al-Nūr, if Allah wills.

(٥١) **وَقالَ اللهُ لا تَتَّخِذُوا إِلهَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ إِنَّما هُوَ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (51) Allah has said, 'Do not take two gods. He is indeed the One God.

The number was emphasized in both places, indicating the attention given to it. For if you had said إِنَّما هو إِله (He is only a God), it would have suggested that you affirmed the Godhead (الإِلهيَّة) but not the Oneness (الوحدانية). فَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ (So fear Me). As if it were said: So I am He, so fear Me and no other.

(٥٢) وَلَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (52) And to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, as creation (خلقاً) and dominion (ملكاً). وَلَهُ الدِّينُ ** and to Him belongs the religion (meaning) obedience (الطاعة) واصِباً. perpetually. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: (meaning) obligatory (واجباً). أَفَغَيْرَ اللهِ تَتَّقُونَ. Will you then fear other than Allah? (٥٣) وَما بِكُمْ مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ فَمِنَ اللهِ. (53) Whatever blessing you have is from Allah. Al-Qummi (said that) the blessing (النعمة) is health (الصِحة), abundance (السعة), and well-being (العافية). And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever does not know that Allah has bestowed a blessing (نعمة) upon him in food or clothing, his deeds have fallen short, and his punishment is near." ثُمَّ إِذا مَسَّكُمُ الضُّرُّ فَإِلَيْهِ تَجْئَرُونَ ** Then, when distress befalls you, you cry out to Him so you do not supplicate except to Him. And al-ja'r (الجؤر) is the raising of the voice in supplication (بالدعاءِ) and seeking aid (والاستغاثة).

(٥٤) ثُمَّ إِذا كَشَفَ الضُّرَّ عَنْكُمْ إِذا فَرِيقٌ مِنْكُمْ بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ. (54) Then, when He removes the distress from you, behold, a part of you ascribe partners to their Lord. (٥٥) لِيَكْفُرُوا بِما آتَيْناهُمْ ** (55) that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them. (This refers to) the blessing of the removal (of distress) from them, as if they intended by their polytheism (بشركهم) the ingratitude (كفران النِّعمة) for the blessing and the denial of its being from Allah. فَتَمَتَّعُوا فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ** (So enjoy yourselves, for soon you will know) is a threat (تهديد) and a warning (وعيد). (٥٦) وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِما لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (56) They set aside a share of what We have provided them for what they do not know. (This refers) to their deities (لآلهتهم) which have no knowledge (لا علم لها), or (to deities) about which they have no knowledge (لا علم لهم بها). نَصِيباً مِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ ** (a share of what We have provided them) (1) from the crops (الزّرُوع) and the livestock (الأنعام). Al-Qummi (said): The Arabs used to set aside a share for the idols (للأصنام) from their crops (زرعهم), camels (إبلهم), and sheep (غنمهم). So Allah refuted them (by saying): تَاللهِ لَتُسْئَلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنْتُمْ تَفْتَرُونَ ** (By Allah, you will surely be questioned concerning what you used to fabricate) (This refers to their fabrication) that they (أنّها) are deities (آلهة) and that they (أنّها) are worthy of being approached for closeness (أهل للتقرب إليها). And this is a warning (وعيد) to them concerning that.

(٥٧) وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ الْبَناتِ. (57) (And they ascribe daughters to Allah) Al-Qummi said: Quraysh said that the angels are the daughters of Allah. سُبْحانَهُ (٢) [This is] a declaration of His purity (تنزيه) from their saying, or an expression of astonishment (تعجب) at it. وَلَهُمْ ما يَشْتَهُونَ ** meaning sons (البَنين).

footnote (١) They seek nearness (*يتقرّبون*) to Him through that, just as one ought to seek nearness to Allah, the Exalted. This is what Allah recounted concerning them in *Surat al-An'am* regarding the tillage (*الحرث*) and other things, and their saying: **هذا لِلَّهِ بِزَعْمِهِمْ وَهذا لِشُرَكائِنا ** (This is for Allah—so they claim—and this is for our partners) (M.N.).

footnote (٢) For they have ascribed to Allah what they dislike for themselves, and this is the utmost degree of ignorance (M.N.).

(٥٨) وَإِذا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُمْ بِالْأُنْثى ظَلَّ وَجْهُهُ مُسْوَدًّا وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ (٥٨) (58) When one of them is brought the news of a female [newborn], his face turns gloomy and he chokes with suppressed anger.

أخبر بولادتها ظَلَ ** صار وَجْهُهُ مُسْوَدًّا من الكآبة والحياءِ من الناسِ وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ ** مملوّ غيظاً من المرأة.

He is informed of her birth. ظَلَ ** his face becomes darkened (مُسْوَدًّا) from grief (الكآبة) and shame (الحياءِ) before the people. وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ ** filled with rage (غيظاً) against the woman (the mother).

(٥٩) يَتَوارى مِنَ الْقَوْمِ مِنْ سُوءِ ما بُشِّرَ بِهِ أَيُمْسِكُهُ عَلى هُونٍ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرابِ أَلا ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ (٥٩) (59) He hides himself from the people because of the evil of the news he has been brought: shall he retain it in humiliation, or bury it in the dust? Behold! Evil is the judgement that they make!

يَتَوارى مِنَ الْقَوْمِ ** يستخفي منهم مِنْ سُوءِ ما بُشِّرَ بِهِ أَيُمْسِكُهُ ** محدثاً نفسه متفكراً في أن يتركه عَلى هُونٍ ** ذلّ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرابِ ** أم يخفيه فيه ويئده (١) **أَلا ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ ** حيث يجعلون لمن تعالى عن الولد ما هذا محلّه عندهم (٢)

يَتَوارى مِنَ الْقَوْمِ ** He conceals himself from them. مِنْ سُوءِ ما بُشِّرَ بِهِ أَيُمْسِكُهُ ** speaking to himself, contemplating whether to leave her (alive). عَلى هُونٍ ** disgrace (ذلّ). أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرابِ ** Or conceal her therein and bury her alive (ويئده) (1). أَلا ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ ** since they assign to the One Who is exalted far above having a child (الولد) that which holds this status (محلّه) in their view (2).

(60) لِلَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ مَثَلُ السَّوْءِ ** (60) For those who do not believe in the Hereafter is the evil attribute The evil attribute (صفة السوء), which is the need for a child (الولد), seeking strength through males (الذكور), hating females (الإِناث), and burying them alive (ووأدهنّ) for fear of poverty (الإِملاق) and disgrace (العار). وَلِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى ** and Allah's is the loftiest attribute. And that is the Divine attributes (الصفات الإِلهيّة), His independence (الغنى) from a consort (الصاحبة) and a child (الولد), and His purity (النزاهة) from the attributes of the created beings (المخلوقين). وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. The One Who is unique (المتفرد) in the perfection of power (القدرة) and wisdom (الحكمة).

(٦١) وَلَوْ يُؤاخِذُ اللهُ النَّاسَ بِظُلْمِهِمْ ** (61) And if Allah were to seize mankind for their wrongdoing, (meaning, for their disbelief (بكفرهم) and their acts of disobedience (ومعاصيهم)) ما تَرَكَ عَلَيْها ** (meaning, upon the earth (على الأرض)) مِنْ دَابَّةٍ ** (absolutely (قط) due to the ill-omen (بشؤم) of their wrongdoing, or any wrongdoing creature (من دابّة ظالمة)) وَلكِنْ يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (so that they may reproduce (كي يتوالدُوا)) فَإِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ. He would not leave upon it any creature, but He defers them until a specified term. Then when their term comes, they will not defer it for an hour, nor advance it.

(٦٢) وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ ما يَكْرَهُونَ ** (62) They assign to Allah what they dislike meaning, what they dislike for themselves, such as daughters, partners (الشركاء) in leadership (الرياسة), belittling the Messengers (الرسل), and the worst of wealth (أراذل الأموال). وَتَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُهُمُ الْكَذِبَ ** and their tongues describe the lie despite that. Al-Qummi says: their lying tongues (ألسنتهم الكاذبة) أَنَّ لَهُمُ الْحُسْنى ** that the best belongs to them. meaning, with Allah, like the saying of one of them: وَلَئِنْ رُجِعْتُ إِلى رَبِّي إِنَّ لِي عِنْدَهُ لَلْحُسْنى (And if I am returned to my Lord, I will surely have the best with Him.) لا جَرَمَ أَنَّ لَهُمُ النَّارَ ** Undoubtedly, the Fire is for them, a refutation of their speech and an affirmation of its opposite. وَأَنَّهُمْ مُفْرَطُونَ ** and they will be sent ahead. meaning, they will be sent forward to the Fire, hastened. And it was read (وقرئ) with a kasrah on the rā’ (مُفْرِطُونَ), meaning those who exceed the bounds (الإفراط) in sins (المعاصي). Al-Qummi: meaning, they will be punished (معذّبون).

footnote (١) الّذي كان من عادة العرب وهو انّ أحدهم كان يحفر حفيرة صغيرة فإذا ولد له أنثى جعلها فيها وحثى عليها التّراب حتّى تموت تحته وكانوا يفعلون ذلك مخافة الفقر عليهنّ فيطمع غير الأكفّاء فيهن م ن. (1) This was the custom of the Arabs, which was that one of them would dig a small pit, and if a female was born to him, he would place her in it and heap earth upon her until she died beneath it. They used to do this fearing poverty for them, lest those who were not suitable (غير الأكفّاء) should desire them. [M.N.]

footnote (٢) وقيل معناه ساء ما يحكمونه في قتل البنات من مساواتهنّ للبنين في حرمة الولادة ولعلّ الجارية خير من الغلام وروي عن ابن عبّاس : لو أطاع الله النّاس في الناس لما كان النّاس لأنّه ليس أحد الّا ويحبّ ان يولد له ذكر ولو كان الجميع ذكوراً لما كان لهم أولاد فيفنى النّاس. (2) And it was said that its meaning is: Evil is what they judge concerning the killing of daughters, regarding their equality with sons in the sanctity of birth, and perhaps the girl (الجارية) is better than the boy (الغلام). And it is narrated from Ibn Abbas: If Allah had obeyed the people concerning people, there would be no people, because there is no one except that he loves to have a male born to him, and if all were males, they would have no children, and people would perish.

(٦٣) تَاللهِ لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا إِلى أُمَمٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** (63) By Allah, We have certainly sent [apostles] to nations before you, but Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them. So they persisted in their ugly deeds and disbelieved in the Messengers. فَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُمُ الْيَوْمَ ** So he is their guardian today, (meaning) their companion (قرينهم) or their helper (ناصرهم), meaning there is no helper for them. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ. and there is a painful punishment for them.

(٦٤) وَما أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ إِلَّا لِتُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ ** (64) We did not send down the Book to you except that you may clarify for them what they differ about, concerning the beginning (المبدء) and the return (المعاد), and the lawful (الحلال) and the unlawful (الحرام). وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةً لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ. and as a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith. (٦٥) وَاللهُ أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَحْيا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها ** (65) Allah sends down water from the sky, and revives the earth with it after its death. He causes various kinds of vegetation to grow therein after its dryness. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ ** There is indeed a sign in that for a people who listen, a listening of reflection (تدبّر) and fairness (انصاف). (٦٦) وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَنْعامِ لَعِبْرَةً (١) (66) There is indeed a lesson for you in the cattle. by which one crosses over from ignorance (الجهل) to knowledge (العلم). نُسْقِيكُمْ مِمَّا فِي بُطُونِهِ ** We give you to drink of that which is in their bellies, The masculine pronoun here is due to the literal wording (اللفظ), while its feminine form in Surat al-Mu'minun is due to the meaning, as it is a collective noun (اسم جمع). مِنْ بَيْنِ فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ لَبَناً ** between excrement and blood, pure milk, which encompass it. خالِصاً ** pure, clear (صافياً), which does not carry the color of blood nor the smell of excrement (الفرث), nor do they mix with it in any way (2). Al-Qummi said: Al-Farath (الفرث) is what is in the rumen (الكرش). سائِغاً لِلشَّارِبِينَ ** palatable to the drinkers. easy to pass down their throats. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "No one chokes (يغصّ) while drinking milk, because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: لَبَناً خالِصاً سائِغاً لِلشَّارِبِينَ.** (pure milk, palatable to the drinkers.)"

وَمِنْ ثَمَراتِ النَّخِيلِ وَالْأَعْنابِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِنْهُ سَكَراً ** (67) And from the fruits of the date-palms and the grapes, you take from it an intoxicant It is said to be wine (خمراً). And al-Qummi (said it means) vinegar (الخلّ). And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it was revealed before the verse of prohibition (آية التحريم), and was thus abrogated by it. And in this, there is an indication that what is meant by it is wine (الخمر). And it has come with both meanings. And based on the intention of (meaning) wine (الخمر), its permissibility (حلّها) at the time of allowance does not necessitate that it be a reproach (عتاباً) or a favor (منّة) before the clarification of its prohibition (تحريمها). And the meaning of abrogation (النّسخ) is the abrogation of silence (نسخ السكوت), so it does not contradict what has been narrated that it was never permissible (حلالاً) at all. And in its juxtaposition with the good provision (الرّزق الحَسَن), there is an alert to its ugliness (قبحها). وَرِزْقاً حَسَناً ** and a good provision such as dates (التمر), raisins (الزّبيب), and date syrup (الدّبس). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ. Indeed, there is certainly a sign in that for a people who apply reason.

(٦٨) وَأَوْحى رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ ** (68) And your Lord inspired the bee: He inspired it (ألهَمَها) and cast [knowledge] into its heart. For its elegant craftsmanship (صنعتها الأنيقة), its subtlety (لطفها) in managing its affair, and its precise observation (دقيق نظرها) are clear evidences (شواهد بيّنة) that Allah the Almighty deposited knowledge (علماً) of that within it. Al-Qummi said: It is the revelation of inspiration (وحي إلهام). And Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The same [meaning]. أَنِ اتَّخِذِي مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً وَمِنَ الشَّجَرِ وَمِمَّا يَعْرِشُونَ ** to build homes in the mountains, and in the trees, and in what they erect. [This refers to] what people erect (يعرش الناس), such as a grapevine (كرم) or a roof (سقفٍ).

(٦٩) ثُمَّ كُلِي مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ ** (69) Then eat of every fruit from every fruit you desire, whether sweet or bitter, فَاسْلُكِي سُبُلَ رَبِّكِ ** the paths He inspired you with for making honey, ذُلُلاً ** docile made subservient, which He made subservient and easy for you, or (it means) you are obedient to what you have been commanded. يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بُطُونِها شَرابٌ ** From its belly comes a drink meaning honey, for it is something that is drunk, مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوانُهُ of diverse hues white, yellow, red, and black, فِيهِ شِفاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ. in which there is a cure for the people.

In al-Kafi and al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Licking honey is a cure for every ailment. Then He recited this Ayah. He said: And it, along with the recitation of the Quran and chewing the tongue (مضغ اللسان), dissolves phlegm (البلغم).

And in al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Three things increase memory and remove phlegm (البلغم), and He mentioned these three.

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: If there is a cure in anything, it is in the incision of the cupper (شرطة الحجّام) and in a drink of honey.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Do not refuse a drink of honey from whoever brings it to you. A narration regarding seeking cure through it has already preceded at the beginning of Surat al-Nisa.

In al-Majma', regarding the bee (النّحل) and honey (العَسَل), there are aspects of consideration (وجوه من الاعتبار), including its uniqueness in that honey comes out of its mouth (فيه), and including the making of the cure from the place of poison, for the bee stings, and including the wonders (البدائع) and marvels (العجائب) that Allah has instilled in it and in its nature. And among the most marvelous of these is that the Glorified One has appointed for every group of them a queen bee (يعسوب) who is their commander (أميرها), who leads them, defends them, manages their affairs, and governs them, and they follow Her and trace Her footsteps. Whenever they lose Her, their order is disrupted, their stability vanishes, and they scatter in every direction (تفرّقت شذر مذر). (1) And to this meaning, I believe, did Ali, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), allude in His saying: "I am the queen bee (يعسوب) of the believers."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, we are the bees (النّحل) to whom Allah revealed: Take homes in the mountains (meaning: We were commanded to take followers (شيعة) from the Arabs), and in the trees (He says: from the non-Arabs (العجم)), and in what they build (He says: from the clients (الموالي)). And that يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بُطُونِها شَرابٌ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوانُهُ ** means the knowledge (العلم) that comes forth from us to you."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): "The bees (النَّحْلِ) are the Imams (الأئمّة), and the mountains (الْجِبالِ) are the Arabs, and the trees (الشَّجَرِ) are the freed clients (الموالي عتاقه), and in what they build (وَمِمَّا يَعْرِشُونَ) means the children and slaves who have not been freed, provided they follow Allah, His Messenger, and the Imams. And the fruits of diverse hues (الثمرات المختلفة ألوانه) are the various types of knowledge (فنون العلم) that the Imams teach their followers (شيعتهم). فِيهِ شِفاءٌ لِلنَّاسِ ** In which there is a cure for the people. And the followers (الشيعة) are the people (الناس), and as for others, Allah knows best what they are. If it were as you claim, that it is the honey which people eat, then no person afflicted with a defect (ذو عاهة) would eat or drink from it except that he would be cured, due to the saying of Allah the Almighty: in which there is a cure for the people, and there is no falsehood in the saying of Allah. Rather, the cure is in the knowledge of the Quran: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ما هُوَ شِفاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ ** We send down in the Quran that which is a cure and a mercy for its people, without doubt or uncertainty. And its people are the Imams of guidance (أئمّة الهدى) about whom Allah said: ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا مِنْ عِبادِنا Then We gave the Book as inheritance to those of Our servants whom We chose.

إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who reflect.

(٧٠) وَاللهُ خَلَقَكُمْ (2) ثُمَّ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ ** with varying appointed times وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرَدُّ إِلى أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ ** its basest and most contemptible part, meaning old age (الهرم) which resembles childhood (الطفوليّة) in the deficiency of strength and intellect. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: It is seventy-five years. And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, from his father عليه السلام: When the servant reaches one hundred years, that is the most abject age (أرذل العمر). And in al-Khisal, similar to it. He said: And it has been narrated that the most abject age (أرذل العمر) is when his intellect is like the intellect of a seven-year-old child. لِكَيْ لا يَعْلَمَ بَعْدَ عِلْمٍ شَيْئاً. (so that he knows nothing after [having had] knowledge.) Al-Qummi said: When he grows old, he does not know what he knew before that. And in al-Kafi, in the narrative concerning the souls (الأرواح), this Ayah is mentioned, then he said: "This is a deficiency in all the souls (الأرواح), and it is not that which causes one to exit the religion of Allah, because the One Who acts upon him is He Who relegates him to the most abject age (أرذل العمر). Thus, he does not know the time for prayer (الصّلوة), nor is he able to perform the night vigil (التّهجّد) by night or by day, nor stand in the row with the people. This is a deficiency in the spirit of faith (روح الإِيمان), and it does not harm him at all." إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ ** of what is proper and fitting for you regarding the measures of lifespans (مقادير الأعمار) قَدِيرٌ ** over not granting you that lifespan.

(٧١) **وَاللهُ فَضَّلَ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي الرِّزْقِ ** (71) And Allah has preferred some of you over others in [respect of] provision.

So among you is the rich and among you is the poor, and among you are masters (موال) who manage their provision and the provision of others, and among you are slaves (مماليك) whom He has placed in a situation contrary to that. **فَمَا الَّذِينَ فُضِّلُوا بِرَادِّي رِزْقِهِمْ ** (So those who have been preferred do not give away their provision)

(meaning) by giving their provision to those whom their right hands possess (عَلى ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُمْ), meaning, to your slaves (مماليككم), so they are equal therein. It was said that its meaning is that the masters (الموالي) and the slaves (المماليك), Allah is the Provider (رازقهم) for all of them, so they are equal in His provision. Thus, the masters should not assume that they provide for the slaves from their own resources, but rather it is the provision of Allah which He causes to flow to them through their hands. And it was said that its meaning is: The masters (الموالي) did not give back what they were provided to your slaves (مماليككم) so that they might be equal in food and clothing. And it was said, rather, its meaning is that Allah made you unequal in provision, so your provision is better than what He provided your slaves (مماليككم), and they are human beings like you. Yet you do not make yourselves equal with them in what Allah has bestowed upon you, nor do you appoint partners for yourselves therein, and you are not pleased with that for yourselves. So how were you pleased to appoint His servants as partners for Him in divinity (الألوهيّة), and you turn in worship and seeking nearness to them just as you turn to Him? **أَفَبِنِعْمَةِ اللهِ يَجْحَدُونَ ** (Will they then deny the blessing of Allah?)

So He made that (act of associating partners) part of the denial (جحود) of the blessing (النعمة). And it was recited in the second person (بالخطاب).

Al-Qummi said: It is not permissible for a man to single himself out with any foodstuff (المأكول) apart from his dependents (عياله). And in al-Jawami': It is narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) saying: "They are only your brothers, so clothe them from what you clothe yourselves with, and feed them from what you feed yourselves with." After that, he was never seen except that his slave's cloak (رداؤه) was his cloak (رداؤه) and his loincloth (إزاره) was his loincloth (إزاره), without any difference (تفاوت).

(٧٢) وَاللهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً ** (72) And Allah has made for you mates from yourselves [Mates] from your own kind (جنسكم) so that you may find comfort (تأنسوا) in them, and so that your children may be like you. And al-Qummi means [by this] that He created Eve (حواء) from Adam. وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَزْواجِكُمْ بَنِينَ وَحَفَدَةً. and made for you sons and grandsons from your mates, In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: The حفدة (grandsons/attendants) are the daughters' sons, and we are the حفدة of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): sons and grandsons (بَنِينَ وَحَفَدَةً). He said: They are the حفدة, and they are the helpers (العون), meaning the sons (البنين). And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): They are the sons-in-law (أختان) of the man through his daughters. And al-Qummi said: The sons-in-law (الأختان). I say: And the meaning of الحافد (the attendant) is the one who is swift in service (الخدمة) and obedience (الطاعة). وَرَزَقَكُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** and provided you with good things. [Provided you] with delights (اللّذائِذ), meaning some of them. أَفَبِالْباطِلِ يُؤْمِنُونَ Do they believe in falsehood It is said that this refers to what they believe regarding the benefit (منفعة) and intercession (شفاعتها) of the idols (الأصنام). وَبِنِعْمَتِ اللهِ هُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ ** and disbelieve in Allah’s blessing? [Disbelieve] in the manifest blessing (بنعمة الله المشاهدة) of Allah, concerning which there is no doubt (شبهة). It is said that their disbelief in it is their attribution of it to the idols (الأصنام), or their prohibition of what Allah has made lawful (أحلّ الله). And it is said that [by] in Allah’s blessing (بِنِعْمَتِ اللهِ) He means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Quran (القرآن), and Islam (الإسلام); that is, they are disbelievers in it and deniers (منكرون) of it.

(٧٣) وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ رِزْقاً مِنَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ شَيْئاً ** (And they worship besides Allah that which does not control any provision for them from the heavens and the earth) meaning, it does not control (يَمْلِكُ) to provide any provision, such as rain and vegetation. وَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ** (nor are they able to) that they should control it (يَمْلِكُوهُ), or they have no ability (اسْتِطَاعَة) themselves. It is said that the pronoun (الضَّمِير) may refer to the disbelievers (الْكُفَّار); meaning, وَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ** (nor are they able to)—they (the disbelievers), despite being alive, are unable to [control] any of that, so how can the inanimate object (الْجَمَاد) [be able]?

(٧٤) فَلا تَضْرِبُوا لِلَّهِ الْأَمْثالَ ** (74) So do not draw comparisons for Allah**

So do not make for Him a likeness (مثلاً) by which you associate partners with Him or by which you compare Him to it, for indeed, striking a comparison (ضرب المثل) is the likening (تشبيه) of one state (حال) to another state (حال). It was said that they used to say that the worship of the king's slaves is more conducive to glorification (التعظيم) than worshipping Him (directly). إِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ** Indeed Allah knows the essence (كنه) of things and the striking of comparisons (ضرب الأمثال), وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ (١) and you do not know.** (1)

footnote (١) And indeed, whoever is a God (*إلهاً*), He is exalted (*منزّه*) above partners (*الشركاء*), and you do not know that, rather you are ignorant of it (*تجهلونه*). And if you were to reflect (*تفكّرتم*), you would know. And it was said that its meaning is: And Allah knows the harm (*الضّرّة*) that is upon you in the worship of other than Him, and you do not know. And if you knew, you would abandon their worship. *م ن.*

(٧٥) ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً عَبْداً مَمْلُوكاً لا يَقْدِرُ عَلى شَيْءٍ وَمَنْ رَزَقْناهُ مِنَّا رِزْقاً حَسَناً فَهُوَ يُنْفِقُ مِنْهُ سِرًّا وَجَهْراً هَلْ يَسْتَوُونَ ** (75) Allah draws a parable: a possessed slave who has no power over anything, and someone whom We have provided with a good provision from Us, and he spends out of it secretly and openly. Are they equal? It is said that its meaning is: If these two are not equal, despite their sharing in species (الجنسيّة) and creaturehood (المخلوقية), then how can the idols, which are the most incapable of creatures, be equal to the Rich One (الغنيّ), the One Who has power over everything? And it is permissible that it is a similitude for the forsaken disbeliever (الكافر المخذول) and the conforming believer (المؤمن الموافق), or the ignorant person (الجاهل) and the knowledgeable teacher (العالم المعلّم). الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ** (All praise belongs to Allah). None other than Him deserves it, let alone worship, because all blessings (النّعم) are from Him. بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (but most of them do not know), so they attribute the blessings (النعم) to other than Him and associate partners with Him.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both), they said: The divorce (طلاقه) or marriage (نكاحه) of the possessed slave (المملوك) is not permissible except with the permission of his master (سيّده). It was asked: "If the master married him off, in whose hand is the divorce (الطلاق)?"
