He said: "In the hand of the master." ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً عَبْداً مَمْلُوكاً لا يَقْدِرُ عَلى شَيْءٍ ** (Allah draws a parable: a possessed slave who has no power over anything). Is divorce (الطلاق) a thing (شيء)? And there are other narrations (أخبار أخر) with this meaning.

(٧٦) وَضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً (١) رَجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُما أَبْكَمُ ** (76) And Allah draws a parable: (1) two men, one of them dumb, He was born mute (أَخْرَسُ), he neither understands nor makes others understand, and he is incapable of anything from the crafts (الصَّنَائِع) and management (التَّدَابِير) due to the deficiency of his intellect (عَقْل). وَهُوَ كَلٌ ** He is a heavy burden (ثِقْل) and a dependent (عِيَال) عَلى مَوْلاهُ ** upon the one who manages his affairs (أَمْر) and supports him (يَعُولُهُ). أَيْنَما يُوَجِّهْهُ ** Wherever his master (مَوْلَاه) sends him concerning an affair (أَمْر), لا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ ** with success (نَجْح) (2) or sufficiency in an important matter (كِفَايَة مُهِمّ). هَلْ يَسْتَوِي هُوَ وَمَنْ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ ** Is he equal to him who enjoins justice And the one who is sound in his senses (سَلِيم الْحَوَاسِ), beneficial (نَفَّاعًا), sufficient (كَافِيًا), possessing guidance (رُشْد) and religiosity (دِيَانَة), so he commands people to justice (الْعَدْل) and goodness (الْخَيْر), وَهُوَ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ and is on a straight path? And he himself is upon a sound religion (دِين قَوِيم) and a righteous conduct (سِيرَة صَالِحَة). And this parable is like the preceding one in its possible interpretations (الِاحْتِمَالَات).

Al-Qummi said: The one who enjoins justice (يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ) is the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِير الْمُؤْمِنِين) and the Imams (الْأَئِمَّة), peace be upon them.

(٧٧) وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (77) To Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth. [It is] that which is hidden from the servants in both of them, and the knowledge of which is concealed. وَما أَمْرُ السَّاعَةِ ** regarding its swiftness and ease إِلَّا كَلَمْحِ الْبَصَرِ ** is but a wink of an eye, like the return of the glance (الطرف) from the top of the pupil (الحدقة) to its bottom, OR (3) it is nearer, because it occurs instantaneously (دفعة). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** Indeed Allah has power over all things. Thus, He is able to resurrect the creatures instantaneously (دفعة) just as He was able to bring them to life gradually (متدرّجاً).

footnote (١) Meaning, Allah clarified a parable (*مثلاً*) in which there is a clarification of the intended meaning, bringing the discourse closer to their understanding. Then He mentioned that parable, saying: a slave (*عبداً مملوكاً*) who has no power over his affairs, and [contrasting him with] one whom We have provided from Us with good provision (*رزقاً حسناً*). He means: and a free man (*حرّا*) whom We have provided and made him possess wealth and blessing (*نعمة*). [Source abbreviation: *م ن*].

footnote (٢) *النجح* and *النجاح* mean the attainment of needs (*الظّفر بالحوائج*). [Source abbreviation: *ص*].

footnote (٣) *أَوْ* is for choice (*التخيير*) or it is in the meaning of *بل* (rather/but).

وَاللهُ أَخْرَجَكُمْ مِنْ بُطُونِ أُمَّهاتِكُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئاً وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ** (78) And Allah brought you forth from the wombs of your mothers while you did not know anything, and He made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts, And He installed in you these instruments for the removal of the ignorance upon which you were born, and for the acquisition of knowledge and acting upon it, لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** so that you may recognize what He bestowed upon you stage after stage, and thus be grateful to Him.

(٧٩) أَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَى الطَّيْرِ ** (79) Have they not regarded the birds And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتّاءِ). مُسَخَّراتٍ ** made subservient (مذلّلات) for flight by what He created for them of wings and the suitable means (الأسباب المواتية) for it. فِي جَوِّ السَّماءِ ** in the air (في الهواء) that is distant from the earth. ما يُمْسِكُهُنَ ** therein إِلَّا اللهُ ** None holds them up therein except Allah, for the heaviness (ثقل) of their bodies necessitates their falling, and there is no tether (علاقة) above them nor any support (دعامة) beneath them to hold them. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith because they are the ones who benefit from them.

(80) وَاللهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ بُيُوتِكُمْ سَكَناً ** (80) Allah has appointed for you homes as a place of rest (a place where you reside during the time of your stay) وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ جُلُودِ الْأَنْعامِ بُيُوتاً ** and He has appointed for you houses made of the hides of cattle (meaning the tents (الخيم) and encampments (المضارب) made from leather (الأدم), camel hair (الوبر), wool (الصُّوف), and goat hair (الشعر)) تَسْتَخِفُّونَها ** which you find light (you find them light; their carrying, transporting, setting up, and pitching are easy for you) يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ ** on the day of your travel (with your baggage and your journey; and it was read with a fathah on the ayn (ظَعَنِكُمْ)) وَيَوْمَ إِقامَتِكُمْ ** and the day of your stay (your settling and your presence [at home]) وَمِنْ أَصْوافِها وَأَوْبارِها وَأَشْعارِها ** and from their wool, fur and hair (Wool (الصوف) is for sheep (الضّأن), fur (الوبر) is for camels (الإبل), and hair (الشعر) is for goats (المعز); as furnishings (أَثاثاً), meaning what is worn and spread out) وَمَتاعاً ** and goods (which is benefited from) إِلى حِينٍ ** for a while. (for a period of time).

Al-Qummi, in the narration of Abu al-Jarud, regarding أَثاثاً ** (furnishings), said: "Wealth (المال)." And regarding وَمَتاعاً ** (goods), he said: "Benefits (المنافع) until a time (إِلى حِينٍ), meaning until their attainment (إلى بلاغها)."

(٨١) وَاللهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِمَّا خَلَقَ ** (81) And Allah has made for you, out of what He has created from trees, mountains, buildings (الأبنية), and other things, shades (ظِلالاً) by which you guard against the heat of the sun. Al-Qummi said: [It means] that by which one seeks shade. وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الْجِبالِ أَكْناناً ** and made for you shelters in the mountains places where you dwell, such as caves (الغيران) and houses carved out within them. وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ سَرابِيلَ ** and made for you shirts garments (ثياباً) made of cotton, linen, wool, and other materials, protecting you from the heat. He sufficed Himself with mentioning one of the two opposites because it indicates the other, and because protection from the heat was more important to them. وَسَرابِيلَ تَقِيكُمْ بَأْسَكُمْ ** and shirts that protect you from your violence meaning coats of mail (الدّروع) and breastplates (الجواشِنَ). And the term سربال encompasses everything that is worn. كَذلِكَ ** Thus like the completion of these blessings that preceded, He completes His blessing upon you يُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْلِمُونَ ** so that you may submit. Meaning: you reflect upon His widespread blessings (نعمه الفاشية) so you believe in Him and submit to His command (حكمه).

**وَاللهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِمَّا خَلَقَ ظِلالاً وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الْجِبالِ أَكْناناً وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ سَرابِيلَ تَقِيكُمُ الْحَرَّ وَسَرابِيلَ تَقِيكُمْ بَأْسَكُمْ كَذلِكَ يُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْلِمُونَ ** (81) And Allah has made for you, out of what He has created, shades, and made for you shelters in the mountains, and made for you shirts that protect you from the heat, and shirts that protect you from your violence. Thus does He complete His blessing upon you so that you may submit.

(٨٢) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا They turn away (أعرضوا) and do not accept from you فَإِنَّما عَلَيْكَ الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ ** (But if they turn away, your duty is only to communicate clearly) And you have already conveyed the message (بلّغت) and discharged your duty (أعذرت). (٨٣) يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ اللهِ ثُمَّ يُنْكِرُونَها وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْكافِرُونَ. (They recognize the blessing of Allah, then they deny it, and most of them are ingrates.) Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "We, by Allah, are the blessing (نعمة الله) of Allah with which He favored His servants, and through us, whoever attained success (فاز) has attained it."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq) from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them) regarding this Ayah, he said: When the Ayah إِنَّما وَلِيُّكُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (Your guardian is only Allah, His Apostle, and those who have faith) was revealed, a group of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gathered in the Mosque of Medina. Some of them said to others, "What do you say about this Ayah?" Some of them replied, "If we disbelieve in this Ayah, we disbelieve in the rest of them. But if we believe, this is humiliation when the son of Abu Talib (peace be upon him) is given authority over us." So they said, "We certainly know that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is truthful in what He says, but we will not take him as a guardian (لا نتولّاه) nor will we obey Ali concerning what He commanded us." He said: Then this Ayah was revealed: يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ اللهِ ثُمَّ يُنْكِرُونَها (They recognize the blessing of Allah, then they deny it). "They recognize" means the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): That He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "They recognized Him, then they denied Him."

(٨٤) وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيداً ** (84) And the day We raise a witness from every nation And He is their prophet and their Imam (إمام) who stands in his place, bearing witness for them and against them regarding faith (الإِيمان) and disbelief (الكفر).

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "For every time and nation, there is an Imam (إمام). Every nation will be resurrected with its Imam (إمامها)."

ثُمَّ لا يُؤْذَنُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** then the faithless will not be permitted [to speak] [They will not be permitted] to offer excuses, since they have no excuse. Thus, the denial of permission indicates that they have neither proof (حجّة) nor excuse (عذر).

وَلا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ ** nor will they be asked to appease [Allah]. They will not be asked to seek satisfaction (يسترضون), since it will not be said to them, "Appease your Lord (ارضوا ربّكم)," [derived] from al-'Utba (العتبى), which is satisfaction (الرّضا).

(85) وَإِذا رَأَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الْعَذابَ ** it became heavy upon them فَلا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمْ وَلا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ ** they are granted respite (يُمْهَلُونَ). (85) When those who did wrong see the punishment, it shall not be lightened for them, nor will they be granted any respite. (86) وَإِذا رَأَى الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا شُرَكاءَهُمْ ** from the idols (الأصنام) and the devils (الشياطين) قالُوا رَبَّنا هؤُلاءِ شُرَكاؤُنَا الَّذِينَ كُنَّا نَدْعُوا مِنْ دُونِكَ we worship them and obey them فَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلَ إِنَّكُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ** Meaning, those whom they worshipped denied them, by Allah making them speak, regarding [the claim] that they were partners of Allah and that they truly worshipped them. Rather, they only worshipped their own desires (أَهْوَاءَهُمْ), like His saying: كَلَّا سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبادَتِهِمْ. (86) When those who ascribed partners [to Allah] see their partners, they will say, 'Our Lord! These are our partners whom we used to invoke besides You.' Then they will cast the word back at them: 'You are indeed liars!'

(٨٧) **وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَى اللهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ السَّلَمَ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** (87) And they will offer submission to Allah on that day, and what they used to fabricate will forsake them.

وَأَلْقَوْا ** And those who wronged offered إِلَى اللهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ السَّلَمَ ** submission (الاستسلام), obedience (الانقياد) to His command. وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** And it was lost from them (وضاع عنهم) and became void (وبطل) ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** of their fabricating that Allah has partners and that they will help them and intercede for them.

(٨٨) الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (88) Those who disbelieved and barred [others] from the way of Allah— By preventing [people] from Islam and urging [them] towards disbelief (الكفر), We shall increase them in punishment due to their barring [others] فَوْقَ الْعَذابِ ** above the punishment which they deserve for their disbelief (كفرهم). (١) بِما كانُوا يُفْسِدُونَ ** because they used to cause corruption, by being corrupters of the people through their barring [them]. Al-Qummi said: كَفَرُوا ** (they disbelieved) after the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, وَصَدُّوا عَنْ ** (and barred [others] from) the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين).

(٨٩) وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيداً عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَجِئْنا بِكَ ** (89) The day We raise in every nation a witness against them from among themselves, and bring you as a witness against these. O Muhammad, as a witness against these. Al-Qummi said: meaning from the Imams. عَلى هؤُلاءِ ** meaning against the Imams (peace be upon them). Thus, the Messenger of Allah is a witness over the Imams (peace be upon them), and they are witnesses over the people. I say: The verification of this meaning has already preceded in Surat al-Baqarah and al-Nisa. وَنَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ تِبْياناً (٢) a eloquent clarification of all things (٣) وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةً وَبُشْرى لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ. and We have sent down the Book to you as a clarification of all things, and as a guidance and mercy and good news for the Muslims.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "We, by Allah, know what is in the heavens and what is in the earth, and what is in Paradise and what is in the Fire, and what is between that." Then he said: "Indeed, that is in the Book of Allah." Then he recited this verse.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah said to Moses: وَكَتَبْنا لَهُ فِي الْأَلْواحِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (7:145) And We wrote for him on the Tablets all kinds of admonition and elaboration of all things. So, we know that He did not write for Moses everything completely. And Allah said to Jesus (peace be upon him): لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ ** (43:63) that I may clarify for you some of that about which you differ. And He said to Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family): وَجِئْنا بِكَ شَهِيداً عَلى هؤُلاءِ وَنَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ تِبْياناً لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ. (16:89 partial) and bring you as a witness against these, and We have sent down the Book to you as a clarification of all things.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, I know what is in the heavens and what is in the earth, and I know what is in Paradise, and I know what is in the Fire, and I know what was and what will be." Then he remained silent for a moment, and seeing that this was difficult for those who heard it from him, he said: "I knew that from the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Indeed, Allah says in it: 'a clarification of all things' (تِبْيَانُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah sent down in the Quran a clarification of all things, so much so, by Allah, He did not leave anything that the servants need, such that no servant can say, 'If only this had been revealed in the Quran,' except that Allah has revealed it therein."

(١) And it was said regarding زِدْناهُمْ ** (We increased them): the vipers and scorpions in the Fire have fangs like tall palm trees. (م ن).

(٢) Meaning, a clarification for every difficult matter. Its meaning is that it clarifies everything needed concerning matters of the Sacred Law (الشَّرْع), for there is nothing that creation needs regarding any matter of their religion except that it is clarified in the Book, either by explicit stipulation (بِالتَّنْصِيصِ عَلَيْهِ) or by reference to that which necessitates knowledge, such as the clarification of the Prophet and the Proofs (الْحُجَج) standing in his place, or the consensus (إِجْمَاع) of the Community (الْأُمَّة). Thus, the ruling for all of them is ultimately derived from the Quran. (م ن).

(٣) Meaning, وَنَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ ** (And We have sent down to you) the Quran is a guidance toward righteousness (الرُّشْد) and a blessing upon creation due to the laws (الشَّرَائِع) and rulings (الْأَحْكَام) contained within it, or because it leads to the blessings of the Hereafter. وَبُشْرى لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ (and good news for the Muslims) means glad tidings for them of everlasting reward (الثَّوَاب) and abiding bliss (النَّعِيم). (م ن).

(٩٠) إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسانِ وَإِيتاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبى ** (90) Indeed Allah enjoins justice and kindness, and giving to kin. and giving the relatives what they need وَيَنْهى عَنِ الْفَحْشاءِ ** that which transgresses the bounds of Allah وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** that which the intellects reject وَالْبَغْيِ and oppression (الْبَغْيِ) is unwarranted aggression against people.

In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Justice (الْعَدْلِ) is fairness (الإِنصاف), and kindness (الإِحسان) is favor (التفضّل).

And al-Qummi said: Justice (الْعَدْلِ) is the testimony that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is the Messenger of Allah. وَالْإِحْسانِ ** (and kindness) is the Commander of the Faithful, and the indecency (الْفَحْشاءِ), the reprehensible (الْمُنْكَرِ), and the oppression (الْبَغْيِ) are so-and-so, and so-and-so, and so-and-so.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) the like of it, except that He said: الْفَحْشاءِ ** (the indecency) is the first one, وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** (the reprehensible) is the second one, وَالْبَغْيِ ** (and the oppression) is the third one. He said: And in the narration of Sa'd from him (peace be upon him): Justice (الْعَدْلِ) is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so whoever obeys him has acted justly. وَالْإِحْسانِ ** (and kindness) is Ali (peace be upon him), so whoever takes him as a guardian (تولّاه) has acted kindly, and the kind-doer (المُحسن) is in Paradise. وَإِيتاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبى ** (and giving to kin) refers to our kinship. Allah commanded the servants to love us and give to us, and He forbade them from the indecency (الْفَحْشاءِ) and the reprehensible (الْمُنْكَرِ)—meaning whoever oppressed us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), and called to someone other than us.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This Ayah was recited in his presence, and he said: "Recite as I tell you: إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسانِ وَإِيتاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبى حقه (Indeed Allah enjoins justice and kindness, and giving to kin their right)." It was said: "We do not recite it this way in the recitation of Zayd." He said: "But we recite it this way in the recitation of Ali (peace be upon him)." It was asked: "So what does He mean by إِيتاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبى ** (giving to kin) their right (حقّه)?" He said: "The handing over (of authority) by an Imam to an Imam after an Imam." وَيَنْهى عَنِ الْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** (and He forbids indecency and the reprehensible). He said: "The guardianship (ولاية) of so-and-so."

يَعِظُكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (He advises you, so that you may take admonition) means: you take admonition (تتعظون).

In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The sum total (جماع) of piety (التقوى) is in His saying: إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسانِ ** (Indeed Allah enjoins justice and kindness) [to the end of] the Ayah. It was said: If there were nothing in the Quran except this Ayah, it would be true to say of it that it is a clarification (تبيان) of everything.

(٩١) وَأَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِ اللهِ إِذا عاهَدْتُمْ وَلا تَنْقُضُوا الْأَيْمانَ بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِها وَقَدْ جَعَلْتُمُ اللهَ عَلَيْكُمْ كَفِيلاً ** (91) And fulfill the covenant of Allah when you make a covenant, and do not break the oaths after confirming them, and you have already made Allah a surety over you. (meaning: a witness (شاهداً) and an observer (رقيباً)) إِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما تَفْعَلُونَ ** Indeed Allah knows what you do. (regarding the breaking of oaths and covenants (في نقض الأيمان والعهود)).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) was revealed, it was among the sayings of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Greet Ali (peace be upon him) with the title Commander of the Faithful (بإمرة المؤمنين)." And among what Allah confirmed upon them on that day was the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to the two of them: "Stand up and greet him with the title Commander of the Faithful (بإمرة المؤمنين)." So they both said: "Is this from Allah or from His Messenger?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "It is from Allah and from His Messenger." Then the Almighty Allah revealed: **وَلا تَنْقُضُوا الْأَيْمانَ بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِها وَقَدْ جَعَلْتُمُ اللهَ عَلَيْكُمْ كَفِيلاً إِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما تَفْعَلُونَ ** (And do not break the oaths after confirming them, and you have already made Allah a surety over you. Indeed Allah knows what you do.) He means by this the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to the two of them, and their saying: "Is this from Allah or from His Messenger?"

And al-Ayyashi narrated what is close to it.

(٩٢) وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَها ** (92) And do not be like her who broke her yarn Like the woman who spun yarn, then broke her yarn مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ** after having spun it firmly after firming (احكام) (1) and twisting أَنْكاثاً ** into untwisted strands. The plural of نكث (2) with a kasrah (بالكسر), which is that whose twisting is broken (يُنْكَث فَتْلُهُ).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَها ** (This refers to) a woman from Banu Tamim ibn Murrah called Raytah bint Ka'b ibn Sa'd ibn Tamim ibn Lu'ayy ibn Ghalib. She was foolish (حمقاء); she would spin hair, and when she had spun it, she would break it, then return and spin it again. So Allah said: كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَها (the Ayah). He (peace be upon him) said: Indeed, Allah Almighty commanded fulfillment (الوفاء) and forbade the breaking of the covenant (نقض العهد), so He struck a parable for them.

تَتَّخِذُونَ أَيْمانَكُمْ دَخَلاً بَيْنَكُمْ ** making your oaths a means of deception among yourselves, (meaning) deceit (دَغَلاً), treachery (خيانة), cunning (مكراً), and deception (خديعة). And that is because they used to conceal treachery (الخيانة) at the time of their covenant, while people relied upon their covenant. And al-dakhala (الدّخل) is when the inner reality (الباطن) is contrary to the outer appearance (الظاهر). Its origin is that something enters that was not originally part of it. أَنْ تَكُونَ أُمَّةٌ هِيَ أَرْبى مِنْ أُمَّةٍ ** lest one community should be more numerous than another. Meaning: Do not break the covenant because one group (جماعة)—namely, the disbelievers of Quraysh (كفرة قريش)—is greater in number (أزيد عدداً) and more abundant in wealth (أوفر مالاً) than another community (أمّة), meaning the community of the believers (جماعة المؤمنين). إِنَّما يَبْلُوكُمُ اللهُ بِهِ ** Allah only tests you with it, He only tests you by their being greater (أربى) to see whether you fulfill the covenant of Allah or are deceived by the multitude of Quraysh, their strength, and their wealth, and the fewness, weakness, and poverty of the believers. وَلَيُبَيِّنَنَّ لَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ما كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ and He will surely make clear to you on the Day of Resurrection that about which you used to differ. (This is) a threat (وعيد) and a warning against opposing the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١) أحكمه (He firmed it) means He perfected it (أتقنه), so it became firm (فاستحكم) and removed it from corruption (الفساد) (Q.).

(٢) From al-nakth (النّكث), meaning the breaking (النّقض) (M.).

(٩٣) وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** submissive (مسلمة), believing (مؤمنة) وَلكِنْ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** by abandonment (بالخذلان) وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ ** by granting success (بالتوفيق) وَلَتُسْئَلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** a question of reproach (تبكيت) and confrontation (مجاراة) (١)

(93) Had Allah wished, He would have surely made you a single nation. But He leads astray whomever He wishes, and guides whomever He wishes, and you will surely be questioned concerning what you used to do.

(٩٤) وَلا تَتَّخِذُوا أَيْمانَكُمْ دَخَلاً بَيْنَكُمْ (Do not make your oaths a means of deception between yourselves) This is an explicit statement prohibiting it, following the implication (التّضمين), for emphasis and exaggeration regarding the ugliness of what is prohibited. فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ (lest a foot should slip) from the clear path (محجّة) of Islam بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِها (after it has been steady) upon it; meaning, lest you stray from guidance (الرّشد) after you were upon guidance (هدىً). It is said: "The foot of so-and-so slipped (زلّ قدم فلان) in such-and-such an affair" when he deviated from what is correct (الصّواب). What is meant is their feet (أقدامهم). It was made singular and indefinite (وحّد ونكّر) to indicate that the slipping (زلل) of a single foot is grave, so how much more so the slipping of many feet? وَتَذُوقُوا السُّوءَ (and you taste the evil consequence) in this world بِما صَدَدْتُمْ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ (because of your barring [others] from the way of Allah) because of your turning away (بصدودكم) or because of your barring (بصدّكم) others from it, for if they were to break the covenant and apostatize, the breaking of it would be taken as a tradition (سنّة) to be followed. وَلَكُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ (and there shall be a great punishment for you) in the Hereafter.

In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): These verses were revealed concerning the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and the pledge of allegiance (البَيعة) to him, when the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Greet Ali (peace be upon him) with the title Commander of the Faithful (بإمرة المؤمنين)."

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He recited [the verse as]: "that there be leaders (أئمّة) who are purer (أزكى) than your leaders (أئمتكم)." It was said: "We recite it as: هِيَ أَرْبى مِنْ أُمَّةٍ (is greater than another community)." So he said: "And what is أربى (arbā)?" and he gestured with his hand and cast it aside. He said: إِنَّما يَبْلُوكُمُ اللهُ بِهِ (Allah only tests you by it), meaning by Ali (peace be upon him), He tests you. بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِها (after it has been steady), meaning after the saying (مقالة) of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) concerning Ali. عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ (from the way of Allah), meaning Ali.

And al-Qummi added: لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً (He would have made you a single community). He said: Upon a single doctrine (مذهب واحد) and a single command (أمر واحد). وَلكِنْ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشاءُ (but He leads astray whomever He wishes), meaning He punishes due to the breaking of the covenant. وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ (and guides whomever He wishes). He said: He rewards.

And al-Ayyashi narrated what is close to it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَها مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ أَنْكاثاً (like the woman who unravels her yarn, breaking it into strands after it has been firmly spun) refers to A'ishah. It is the breaking (نكث) of her oath (إيمانها).

(١) Al-Tabkit (التبكيت) is reprimand (التقريع) and overcoming by argument (الغلبة بالحجّة). Jārāhu mujārātan wa jirā’an (جاراه مجاراة وجراءً) means he ran with him (جرى معه). (ق.)

(٩٥) وَلا تَشْتَرُوا بِعَهْدِ اللهِ ** (95) (And do not sell Allah’s covenant) And do not exchange the covenant of Allah and the pledge (بيعة) to the Messenger of Allah for a small price (ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً), a slight gain (عَرْضاً يسيراً) from the enjoyment (متاع) of the world. Indeed, what is with Allah of the reward (الثواب) for fulfilling the covenant (الوفاء بالعهد) is better for you, if you are knowing (تَعْلَمُونَ).

(٩٦) ما عِنْدَكُمْ ** of the enjoyment of this world (متاع الدنيا) يَنْفَدُ ** meaning, it ceases and vanishes (ينقضي ويفنى). And what is with Allah of the treasures of His mercy (خزائن رحمته) is everlasting (باقٍ), it does not perish (لا ينفد). وَلَنَجْزِيَنَ ** And it was recited with the nūn (بالنّون). الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا أَجْرَهُمْ بِأَحْسَنِ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ.**

مَا عِنْدَكُمْ يَنْفَدُ وَمَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ بَاقٍ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّ الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا أَجْرَهُمْ بِأَحْسَنِ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (What is with you will be exhausted, and what is with Allah is everlasting. And We will surely pay those who are patient their reward by the best of what they used to do.)

(٩٧) مَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَياةً طَيِّبَةً ** (97) Whoever —male or female— does a righteous deed, and is a believer, We shall certainly make him live a good life, in the world (الدنيا), he lives a good life (عيشاً طيباً). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It is contentment (القنوع) with what Allah has provided Him. And in Nahj al-Balaghah: He (peace be upon him) was asked about it, and he said: It is contentment (القناعة). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: It is contentment (القناعة) and satisfaction (الرضا) with what Allah has apportioned. وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ and We shall certainly pay them their reward بِأَحْسَنِ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** by the best of what they used to do. of obedience (الطاعة).

(٩٨) فَإِذا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ ** (98) When you recite the Quran, If you intend to recite it, فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ الرَّجِيمِ ** (seek the protection of Allah from the outcast Satan.) So ask Allah that He grant you refuge from his whispers (وساوسه), lest he whisper to you during the recitation.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "How should I say it?" He said: "You say: أستعيذ بالله السميع العليم من الشيطان الرجيم (I seek refuge with Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing, from the rejected Satan)." He said: "al-Rajim (الرّجيم)" is the most wicked of the devils (الشياطين).

And in Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from Sudayr, who said: I prayed the Maghrib prayer behind Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), and He sought refuge aloud: أعوذ بالله السميع العليم من الشيطان الرجيم (I seek refuge with Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing, from the rejected Satan) and أعوذ بالله أن يحضرون (I seek refuge with Allah from their presence), then He recited aloud: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful). And the general public (العامّة) narrated from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: I recited to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and I said: أعوذ بالله من الشيطان الرَّجِيمِ (I seek refuge with Allah from the rejected Satan). Thus did Gabriel make me recite it, from the Pen (القلم), from the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ). And the explanation of seeking refuge (الاستعاذة) has already preceded at the beginning of the book.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): If you recite بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful), then you need not worry about not seeking refuge (تستعيذ).

(٩٩) إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطانٌ dominance (تسلّط) and authority (ولاية) عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ for they do not obey his commands. (99) Indeed, he has no authority over those who have faith and who rely on their Lord.

(١٠٠) إِنَّما سُلْطانُهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَتَوَلَّوْنَهُ they love him and obey him وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِهِ مُشْرِكُونَ. (100) His authority is only over those who take him as a guardian, and those who ascribe partners to Him.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "By Allah, he is given authority over the believer concerning his body, but he is not given authority over his religion (دينه). Indeed, he was given authority over Ayyub (أيّوب) so that his physical form (خلقه) was disfigured, but he was not given authority over his religion (دينه)." And He said concerning those who ascribe partners to Allah: مُشْرِكُونَ he is given authority over their bodies (أبدانهم) and over their religions (أديانهم).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "He does not have the power to remove them from the guardianship (الولاية). As for sins (الذّنوب) and similar matters, he affects them (ينال منهم) just as he affects others." Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated the like of it.

(101) وَإِذا بَدَّلْنا آيَةً مَكانَ آيَةٍ (101) When We replace an ayah with another ayah by abrogation (بالنسخ), وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يُنَزِّلُ and Allah knows best what He sends down regarding the expediencies (المصالح). For perhaps what constitutes an expediency (مصلحة) at one time constitutes a corruption (مفسدة) at another. And this is an interjection intended to rebuke the disbelievers concerning their saying or their state. قالُوا they say —meaning the disbelievers (الكفّار)— إِنَّما أَنْتَ مُفْتَرٍ ‘You are only a fabricator.’ fabricating a lie against Allah, (saying): You command something, then it appears to you (a change of mind), so you forbid it.

Al-Qummi said: When an Ayah was abrogated, they would say to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, "You are a fabricator (مفتر)." So Allah refuted them: بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ Rather, most of them do not know the wisdom of the rulings (الأحكام), nor do they distinguish error (الخطأ) from correctness (الصَّواب).

(١٠٢) قُلْ نَزَّلَهُ رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ ** (102) Say: 'The Holy Spirit has brought it down' meaning جبرئيل مِنْ رَبِّكَ بِالْحَقِ ** (from your Lord with the truth) invested with wisdom لِيُثَبِّتَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (that He may make firm those who have faith) upon faith, [confirming] that it is the speech of Allah. For when they hear the sincere advisor (النّاصِح) and reflect upon what it contains of the observance of righteousness (الصّلاح) and wisdom (الحِكمة), their beliefs become firm and their hearts are reassured. وَهُدىً وَبُشْرى لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (and as a guidance and good news for the Muslims.) those who submit to His decree.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ ** (Rūḥ al-Qudus) is جبرئيل (Jibrīl), and القدس (al-Qudus) means the Pure (الطّاهر). لِيُثَبِّتَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (that He may make firm those who have faith) refers to the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, blessed and exalted is He, created رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ ** (Rūḥ al-Qudus), and He did not create a creation closer to Him than it, yet it is not the most honored of His creation to Him. When Allah wills a matter, He casts it to it, and it casts it to the stars (النجُوم), and it proceeds thereby.

(١٠٣) وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّما يُعَلِّمُهُ بَشَرٌ لِسانُ الَّذِي يُلْحِدُونَ إِلَيْهِ ** (103) We certainly know that they say, 'It is only a human that teaches him.' The tongue of him whom they incline toward They attribute the teaching to him and incline their saying away from uprightness toward him. And it was read with the fathah on the yā' and the hā' (يَلْحَدُونَ). أَعْجَمِيٌ ** (is foreign) meaning: not clear/unclear. **وَهذا ** (while this [Qur'an] is) لِسانٌ عَرَبِيٌّ مُبِينٌ (an eloquent Arabic tongue) (1) possessing clarity and eloquence.

Al-Qummi (said): لِسانُ الَّذِي يُلْحِدُونَ إِلَيْهِ ** (The tongue of him whom they incline toward) is the tongue of Abu Fukayhah, the mawla (مولى) of Ibn Al-Hadrami. He was foreign (أعجمِيّ) in speech (اللِّسان). He had followed the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and believed in him, and he was one of the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). So Quraysh said: "By Allah, this one is teaching Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم; he taught him with his tongue."

footnote (١) Meaning: If the Arabs are incapable of producing its like, even though it is in their language, then how could a foreigner (*الأعجميّ*) produce its like? م ن.

(١٠٤) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ ** (104) Indeed those who do not believe in the signs of Allah They do not believe that they are from Allah. لا يَهْدِيهِمُ اللهُ ** Allah does not guide them, He does not show kindness (لطف) to them and He forsakes them. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** and there is a painful punishment for them. in the Hereafter. (١٠٥) إِنَّما يَفْتَرِي الْكَذِبَ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ ** (105) Only those who do not believe in the signs of Allah fabricate lies, because they do not fear a punishment that would deter them from it. This is a rebuttal to their saying: **إِنَّما أَنْتَ مُفْتَرٍ ** (You are only a fabricator). Meaning: The fabrication of lies is only fitting for one who does not believe in Allah, because faith prevents lying, وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكاذِبُونَ (and it is they who are the liars).

(١٠٦) مَنْ كَفَرَ بِاللهِ (١) مِنْ بَعْدِ إِيمانِهِ إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمانِ ** (Whoever disbelieves in Allah after his belief—barring someone who is compelled [to utter a word of disbelief] while his heart is at rest with faith) his creed (عقيدته) did not change وَلكِنْ مَنْ شَرَحَ بِالْكُفْرِ صَدْراً ** (but those who open up their breasts to disbelief) believed in it and felt content (طاب به نفساً) with it فَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (upon them shall be the wrath of Allah, and there shall be a great punishment for them) since there is no crime (جرم) greater than his crime.

Al-Qummi (said regarding): إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمانِ ** (barring someone who is compelled [to utter a word of disbelief] while his heart is at rest with faith): He is Ammar ibn Yasir. Quraysh seized him in Mecca and tortured him with fire until he gave them what they wanted with his tongue, while his heart was at rest with faith. And His saying: وَلكِنْ مَنْ شَرَحَ بِالْكُفْرِ صَدْراً ** (but those who open up their breasts to disbelief): He is Abdullah ibn Sa'd ibn Abi Sarh ibn al-Harith ibn Lu'ayy. He said: And he was a governor (عاملاً) for Uthman ibn Affan over Egypt (مصر).

I say: The story of Ammar, according to what the exegetes (المفسّرون) narrated concerning the occasion of the revelation (شأن نزول) of this verse, is: that Quraysh compelled him and his parents, Yasir and Sumayyah, to apostatize (الارتداد). His parents refused, so they killed them, and they were the first two martyrs (قتيلين) in Islam. And Ammar gave them what they wanted with his tongue, being compelled (مكرهاً). So it was said: "O Messenger of Allah, Ammar has disbelieved." He said: "No, Ammar is filled with faith from the top of his head (قرنه) to his feet (مقدمه), and faith has mingled with his flesh and blood." Then Ammar came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while he was weeping. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم began wiping his eyes and said: "What is wrong with you? If they return to you, then return to them with what you said."

In al-Kafi: It was said to al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "People narrate that Ali عليه السلام said upon the pulpit (منبر) of Kufa: 'O people, you will be called upon to curse me, so curse me. Then you will be called upon to disavow me (البراءة منّي), so do not disavow me.'" He said: "How often people lie about Ali عليه السلام." He said: "He only said: 'You will be called upon to curse me, so curse me. Then you will be called upon to disavow me, and indeed I am upon the religion (دين) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.' And he did not say: 'Do not disavow me.'" The questioner then asked him: "What if I choose death instead of disavowal (البراءة)?" He said: "By Allah, that is not incumbent upon him (ما ذاك عليه), and he has no choice except what Ammar ibn Yasir proceeded upon when he was compelled and his heart was at rest with faith." Then Allah revealed concerning him: **إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمانِ ** (barring someone who is compelled [to utter a word of disbelief] while his heart is at rest with faith). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله then said to him: "O Ammar, if they return, then return [to what you said]. Allah has indeed revealed your excuse and commanded you to return if they return."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: the like of it. And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked: "Is the stretching of necks (مدّ الرقاب) [i.e., execution] more beloved to you, or the disavowal (البراءة) of Ali عليه السلام?" He replied: "The dispensation (الرخصة) is more beloved to me. Have you not heard the saying of Allah concerning Ammar: إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمانِ. (barring someone who is compelled [to utter a word of disbelief] while his heart is at rest with faith.)"

footnote (١) Al-Zajjaj said: His saying **مَنْ كَفَرَ بِاللهِ ** (Whoever disbelieves in Allah) is in the nominative case (*موضع رفع*) as an appositive (*البدل*) of *al-kādhibīn* (*الكاذبين*). It is not permissible for it to be nominative by virtue of being a subject (*الابتداء*) because there is no predicate (*خبر*) here for the subject, for His saying **مَنْ كَفَرَ بِاللهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِيمانِهِ إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمانِ ** is not a complete sentence (*كلام تامّ*). And His saying **فَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ مِنَ اللهِ ** (upon them shall be the wrath of Allah) is the predicate (*خبر*) of His saying **مَنْ شَرَحَ بِالْكُفْرِ صَدْراً ** (but those who open up their breasts to disbelief). And the Kufans (*الكوفيّون*) said: **مَنْ كَفَرَ ** is a conditional particle (*شرط*), and its answer (*جوابه*) is indicated by the answer of **مَنْ شَرَحَ **. It is as if it were said: "Whoever disbelieves, upon him is the wrath of Allah." M. N.

(١٠٧) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ اسْتَحَبُّوا الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا عَلَى الْآخِرَةِ ** (That is because they preferred the life of the world to the Hereafter) because they preferred it over the latter, وَأَنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكافِرِينَ (and because Allah does not guide the faithless lot) meaning, those who are faithless (الْكافِرِينَ) in His knowledge, towards that which necessitates the firmness of faith (الإِيمان).

(108) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللهُ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ وَسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصارِهِمْ ** (108) They are the ones whose hearts, hearing, and sight Allah has sealed. so they were prevented from perceiving the truth. وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْغافِلُونَ ** And it is they who are the oblivious. the complete ones in obliviousness, since they were heedless of reflecting upon the consequence of their affair.

(109) **لا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** (109) Undoubtedly, it is they who will be the losers in the Hereafter. Since they wasted their lives by spending them on that which led to the everlasting punishment.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) used to call his companions, so whoever Allah willed good for, he heard and recognized what He was calling him to, and whoever He willed evil for, He sealed his heart, so he neither heard nor understood. And this is His saying, the Almighty: أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللهُ ** the Ayah.

(١١٠) ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما فُتِنُوا ** (110) Then indeed your Lord, to those who emigrated after they were persecuted, They were tormented for the sake of Allah and compelled to disbelief (الكفر), so they gave some of what was desired from them so that they might be safe from their evil, like Ammar (كعمّار). And it was recited with the fā (الفاءِ) and the tā (التّاءِ) having a fatḥah (فتح) [i.e., فَتَنُوا]. جاهَدُوا وَصَبَرُوا ** upon the struggle (الجهاد) and what afflicted them of hardships (المشاقّ). إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنْ بَعْدِها ** after the persecution (الافتقان), the struggle (الجهاد), and the patience (الصّبر), لَغَفُورٌ for what they did previously رَحِيمٌ ** He bestows favor upon them as a reward for their hardships (مشاقّهم). لَغَفُورٌ ** is the predicate (خبر) of both the first and the second inna (أنّ) entirely. And the like of this repetition (التكرير) is frequent in the Quran. And thumma (ثُمَّ) is for the remoteness of the state of these people from the state of those people.

(١١١) يَوْمَ تَأْتِي كُلُّ نَفْسٍ تُجادِلُ عَنْ نَفْسِها ** (The day every soul will come pleading for itself) i.e., its very self will argue on its behalf, offer excuses for it, and strive for its deliverance. The affair of others does not concern it, so it says, "My soul! My soul!" وَتُوَفَّى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما عَمِلَتْ ** (and every soul will be paid in full for what it has done) the recompense for what it has done وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ. (and they will not be wronged.)

(١١٢) وَضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً قَرْيَةً كانَتْ آمِنَةً مُطْمَئِنَّةً يَأْتِيها رِزْقُها رَغَداً مِنْ كُلِّ مَكانٍ فَكَفَرَتْ بِأَنْعُمِ اللهِ فَأَذاقَهَا اللهُ لِباسَ الْجُوعِ وَالْخَوْفِ بِما كانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ. (112) And Allah cites a parable: a town which was secure and peaceful, its provision coming to it abundantly from every place. But it became ungrateful for Allah’s bounties, so Allah made it taste the garment of hunger and fear because of what they used used to do.

[The parable is cited] for every people upon whom Allah bestowed blessings, but the blessing made them insolent (أَبْطَرَتْهُمْ), so they disbelieved in it, and Allah sent down His retribution upon them. a town which was secure and peaceful, whose people were not disturbed by fear, its provision came to it abundantly from every place (مِنْ كُلِّ مَكَانٍ), from its surrounding areas. But it became ungrateful for Allah’s bounties, so Allah made it taste the garment of hunger and fear And it was recited (وقرئ) with the nasb (نَصْب) of al-khawf (الخوف) [meaning wa al-khawfa]. The tasting (الذّوق) is used metaphorically for perceiving the effect of harm, and the garment (اللباس) is used for what enveloped them and covered them, namely hunger [and fear]. because of what they used used to do.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning a people who had a river called al-Billiyān. Their lands were fertile and abundant in goodness. They used to clean themselves (يَسْتَنْجُونَ) with dough (العَجين), saying, "It is softer for us." So they disbelieved in the bounties of Allah and treated the blessing of Allah lightly. Thus, Allah withheld al-Billiyān from them, and they suffered drought (جَدَبُوا) until Allah compelled them to need what they used to clean themselves with, to the extent that they were dividing it among themselves.

And Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "My father used to dislike wiping his hand with a towel if there was any food on it, out of reverence for it, unless he licked it, or if there was a child beside him, He would let the child lick it for him." He (peace be upon him) said: "And I find a small amount falling from the dining cloth (الخِوَان), so I search for it, and the servant laughs." Then he said: "The people of a town among those who were before you—Allah had expanded [provision] for them until they became rebellious (طَغَوْا). Some of them said: 'If we took some of this fine flour (النَّقِيّ) and used it to clean ourselves (نَسْتَنْجِي), it would be softer for us than stones (الحِجَارَة).' He said: When they did that, Allah sent upon their land creatures smaller than locusts, and they left nothing that Allah had created, whether tree or otherwise, except that they consumed it. The hardship reached them to the point that they turned to what they used to clean themselves with and ate it. And this is the town about which Allah said:

ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً قَرْيَةً كانَتْ آمِنَةً مُطْمَئِنَّةً Allah cites a parable: a town which was secure and peaceful, up to His saying: بِما كانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ (because of what they used used to do).

(١١٣) وَلَقَدْ جاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِنْهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمُ الْعَذابُ وَهُمْ ظالِمُونَ. (113) Certainly there came to them an apostle from among themselves, but they belied him. So the punishment seized them while they were wrongdoers. (١١٤) فَكُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ حَلالاً طَيِّباً وَاشْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ. (114) So eat of what Allah has provided you, lawful and good, and thank the blessing of Allah, if it is Him that you worship. (١١٥) **إِنَّما حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةَ وَالدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ الْخِنْزِيرِ وَما أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللهِ بِهِ فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ باغٍ وَلا عادٍ فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (115) He has only forbidden you carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and that over which other than Allah has been invoked. But should someone be compelled, without desiring it or exceeding the limit, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. Its exegesis has already preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

(116) وَلا تَقُولُوا لِما تَصْفُ أَلْسِنَتُكُمُ الْكَذِبَ هذا حَلالٌ وَهذا حَرامٌ. (116) And do not say, for what your tongues describe falsely, 'This is lawful, and this is unlawful,'

Al-Qummi said: It is what the Jews used to say: ما فِي بُطُونِ هذِهِ الْأَنْعامِ خالِصَةٌ لِذُكُورِنا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلى أَزْواجِنا. (What is) in the bellies of these cattle is exclusively for our males and forbidden to our wives.

It was said: Meaning, do not declare lawful (تُحَلِّلُوا) nor declare unlawful (تُحَرِّمُوا) merely based on a saying that your tongues utter, without proof (حُجَّة) or textual evidence (نَصّ). And the description of their tongues with falsehood (الْكَذِب) is an exaggeration (مُبَالَغَة) in describing their speech with falsehood, as if the reality of falsehood (حَقِيقَةُ الْكَذِب) was unknown, and their tongues describe it and define it (تُعَرِّفُهَا) through this speech of theirs, like their saying: "Her face describes beauty," and "Her eye describes magic (السِّحْر)."

لِتَفْتَرُوا عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ so that you may fabricate a lie against Allah.

is of the nature of a justification (التَّعْلِيل) that the purpose does not entail.

إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ لا يُفْلِحُونَ. Indeed, those who fabricate a lie against Allah will not be felicitous.

(١١٧) مَتاعٌ قَلِيلٌ (A brief enjoyment) Meaning: that for which they fabricate is a small benefit that will soon be cut off. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (and there is a painful punishment for them) in the Hereafter.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If the servant commits a major sin (كبيرة) from the major sins (كبائر المعاصي) or a minor sin (صغيرة) from the minor sins (صغائر المعاصي) which Allah has forbidden, he is outside of Iman (الإيمان), and the name of Iman falls away from him, while the name of Islam remains fixed upon him. If he repents and seeks forgiveness, he returns to Iman (الإيمان), and this does not take him out to Kufr (الكفر), denial (الجحود), or deeming lawful (الاستحلال). But if he says concerning the lawful (الحلال), 'This is unlawful (حرام),' and concerning the unlawful (الحرام), 'This is lawful (حلال),' and he adheres to that belief (دان بذلك), then, according to us, he is outside of Iman (الإيمان) and Islam (الإسلام) and into Kufr (الكفر). And he is like a man who entered the Sacred Precinct (الحرم), then entered the Ka'bah, and committed an offense (حَدَثاً) inside the Ka'bah. So, he is expelled from the Ka'bah and from the Sacred Precinct, and his neck is struck, and he goes to the Fire (النّار). The narrative (الحديث).

(١١٨) وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا ما قَصَصْنا عَلَيْكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (118) And to those who became Jews We forbade what We recounted to you before, meaning, in Surat al-An'am with His saying: وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا كُلَّ ذِي ظُفُرٍ الآية (And to those who became Jews We forbade every animal with unseparated toes) the Ayah. وَما ظَلَمْناهُمْ ** And We did not wrong them by the prohibition وَلكِنْ كانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ ** but they used to wrong themselves where they committed what they were punished for because of it. And in it is an indication that the prohibition upon them was for punishment, not for detriment (المضرّة).

(١١٩) ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السُّوءَ بِجَهالَةٍ ** (119) Then indeed your Lord, to those who commit evil out of ignorance, being ignorant (جاهلين), not reflecting upon the consequence (للعاقبة). ثُمَّ تابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنْ بَعْدِها ** and then repent after that and reform, indeed your Lord is thereafter after that, meaning after the repentance (التّوبة). لَغَفُورٌ ** all-forgiving, for that evil (السّوء). رَحِيمٌ ** all-merciful. He rewards for the turning back (الإِنابة).

(١٢٠) إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ كانَ أُمَّةً قانِتاً لِلَّهِ حَنِيفاً. (120) Indeed, Abraham was a nation, obedient to Allah, a ḥanīf.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "And the Ummah (الأمّة) is one person or more, just as Allah said," and He recited the verse.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "That is because he was upon a religion that no one else was upon besides him, so it was as if he were a single nation (أمّة واحدة). As for قانِتاً ** (obedient), it means the obedient one (المطيع), and as for al-Hanif (الحنيف), it means the Muslim (المسلم)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It is something by which Allah favored him." And narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "The world and what is in it contained none but one person who worshipped Allah. Had there been anyone else with him, He would have surely joined him to him, since He says: **إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ كانَ أُمَّةً ** and the rest of the verse. So, He expressed by that what Allah willed. Then, Allah comforted him with Isma'il and Ishaq, so they became three."

وَلَمْ يَكُ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (and he was not one of the polytheists) is a refutation of Quraysh regarding what they claimed: that they were upon the religion (ملّة) of Abraham (peace be upon him).

(١٢١) شاكِراً لِأَنْعُمِهِ ** (121) [He was] grateful for His bounties. [Grateful] for the bounties of Allah, acknowledging them. It is narrated: He would not eat his meal except with his guest. اجْتَباهُ ** He chose him He selected him. وَهَداهُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ and guided him to a straight path. To the clear path. (١٢٢) وَآتَيْناهُ فِي الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةً ** (122) And We gave him a good [share] in the world, By making him beloved to people, so much so that the followers of [various] religions (أرباب الملل) befriended him and praised him, and He provided him with good children and a long life in abundance and obedience. وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ** and in the Hereafter He will indeed be among the righteous. [He will indeed be] among the people of Paradise, just as he requested in His saying: وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ (and join me with the righteous).

(١٢٣) ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ ** O Muhammad أَنِ اتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ حَنِيفاً وَما كانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (123) Then We revealed to you: 'Follow the creed of Abraham, a ḥanīf, and he was not one of the polytheists.'

It was said regarding this ثُمَّ (then) that it is an exaltation of the status of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and an announcement that the best of the honors (الكرامة) granted to the Friend of Allah (خليل الله) is the following of his creed by our Prophet, as it indicates the remoteness of this attribute (النّعت) in rank among the rest of the attributes with which Allah praised him.

In Miṣbāḥ al-Sharī‘ah, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: There is no path for the intelligent (الأكياس) (1) among the believers safer than emulation (الاقتداء), because it is the clearest methodology (المنهج). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ أَنِ اتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ حَنِيفاً ** (Then We revealed to you: 'Follow the creed of Abraham, a ḥanīf'). For if there were a straighter course (مسلك) for the religion of Allah, the Exalted, than emulation (الاقتداء), He would have urged His saints (أولياءه) and His prophets (أنبياءه) toward it.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali عليهما السلام: No one is upon the creed of Abraham except us and our followers (شيعتنا), and the rest of the people are disassociated (براء) from it.

(١٢٤) إِنَّما جُعِلَ السَّبْتُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فِيما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ. (124) The Sabbath was only appointed for those who differed about it, and indeed your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ.

Al-Qummi said: That was because Moses (peace be upon him) commanded his people to devote themselves to Allah one day out of every seven days, which Allah appointed for them, and they are الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ. (those who differed about it).

I say: Their story has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf.

(١٢٤) **ادْعُ إِلى سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ ** (125) Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good advice, and debate with them in the best manner. Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are guided.

—with the firm, correct discourse (المقالة المحكمة الصحيحَة) that clarifies the truth and removes doubt (الشبهة). This is for the elite (الخواص). وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ ** (and good advice) means the convincing addresses (الخطابات المقنعة) and beneficial lessons (العبر النّافعة) through which it is not hidden from them that you are sincerely advising them and benefiting them therein. This is for the common people (العوامّ). وَجادِلْهُمْ (2) بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** (and debate with them in the best manner) means by the method (الطريقة) which is the best of the methods of debating (المجادلة). This is for the obstinate (المعاندين) and the deniers (الجاحدين).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by the Quran.

In al-Ihtijaj and Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: قُلْ هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (Say, 'Produce your evidence, should you be truthful') from Surat al-Baqarah: The debate (الجدال) concerning religion was mentioned in the presence of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imams (peace be upon them) had forbidden it. Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "It was not forbidden absolutely, but rather He forbade the debate (الجدال) that is not in the best manner (بغير الّتي هي أحسن). Do you not hear the Almighty's saying: وَلا تُجادِلُوا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ (And do not dispute with the People of the Book except in the best manner) and His saying: ادْعُ إِلى سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ وَجادِلْهُمْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ (Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good advice, and debate with them in the best manner)? Thus, the debate in the best manner (الجدال بالتي هي أحسن) has been commanded by the scholars of religion, while the debate not in the best manner (الجدال بغير التي هي أحسن) is forbidden (محرّم), which Allah has forbidden for our followers (شيعتِنا). And how could Allah forbid debate (الجدال) entirely when He says: وَقالُوا لَنْ يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَنْ كانَ هُوداً أَوْ نَصَارى ** (And they say, 'No one will enter paradise except for someone who is a Jew or a Christian')? Allah the Almighty said: تِلْكَ أَمانِيُّهُمْ قُلْ هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (These are their aspirations. Say, 'Produce your evidence, should you be truthful'). So, He made the knowledge of truthfulness (الصدق) and faith (الايمان) dependent upon evidence (البرهان). And is evidence (البرهان) brought forth except through debate in the best manner (الجدال بالتي هي أحسن)?"

It was asked: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what is the debate in the best manner (الجدال بالتي هي أحسن) and what is the one that is not in the best manner (التي ليست بأحسن)?" He said: "As for the debate not in the best manner (الجدال بغير التي هي أحسن), it is that you debate with a proponent of falsehood (مبطلاً), and he presents a falsehood to you, and you do not refute it with an argument (حجّة) that Allah has established, but instead, you deny a truth (حقّاً)—intending thereby that the proponent of falsehood (المبطل) should use it to support his falsehood—so you deny that truth (الحقّ) for fear that he might have an argument (حجّة) against you concerning it, because you do not know how to escape from it. That is forbidden (حرام) for our followers (شيعتنا), lest they become a trial (فتنة) for their weak brethren and for the proponents of falsehood (المبطلين). As for the proponents of falsehood, they will use the weakness of the weak among you, when he attempts to debate them and his argument (حجّة) fails in his hand, as evidence for their falsehood. As for the weak, their hearts will be grieved when they see the weakness of the proponent of truth (المحقّ) in the hand of the proponent of falsehood (المبطل).

As for the debate in the best manner (الجدال بالتي هي أحسن), it is what Allah commanded His Prophet to use when debating those who denied the Resurrection after death and Allah the Almighty's bringing it back to life. Allah spoke to him, relating from the denier: وَضَرَبَ لَنا مَثَلاً وَنَسِيَ خَلْقَهُ قالَ مَنْ يُحْيِ الْعِظامَ وَهِيَ رَمِيمٌ (And he draws a parable for Us and forgets his own creation. He says, 'Who will revive the bones when they are decayed?') And Allah said in refutation of him: قُلْ ** (Say), O Muhammad, يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنْشَأَها أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ خَلْقٍ عَلِيمٌ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ ناراً (He will revive them who produced them the first time, and He has knowledge of all creation. He who produced fire for you from the green tree) until the end of the Surah.

So Allah intended for His Prophet to debate the proponent of falsehood (المبطل) who said: "How is it possible for these bones to be resurrected when they are decayed (رميم)?" So Allah said: قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنْشَأَها أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (Say, 'He will revive them who produced them the first time'). Is He Who originated it from nothing incapable of restoring it after it has decayed? Rather, its origination is more difficult in your estimation than its restoration. Then He said: الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ ناراً فَإِذا أَنْتُمْ مِنْهُ تُوقِدُونَ ** (He who produced fire for you from the green tree, and behold, you kindle from it), meaning: when He extracts the hot fire (النّار الحارة) from the fresh, green tree (الشجر الأخضر الرّطب), He makes known to you that He is more capable of restoring what has decayed. Then He said: أَوَلَيْسَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِقادِرٍ عَلى أَنْ يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُمْ بَلى وَهُوَ الْخَلَّاقُ الْعَلِيمُ (Is not He who created the heavens and the earth able to create the likes of them? Yes, indeed! He is the All-creator, the All-knowing). If the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater and further removed in your imaginations and estimations—that you should deem Him capable of it—than the restoration of what has decayed, then how did you permit Allah to create this thing which is more wondrous in your view and more difficult in your estimation, yet you did not permit Him to do what is easier in your view, which is the restoration of what has decayed?

Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "This is the debate in the best manner (الجدال بالّتي هي أحسن), because in it is the cutting off of the excuse of the disbelievers (الكافرين) and the removal of their doubt (شبهتهم). As for the debate not in the best manner (الجدال بغير التي هي أحسن), it is that you deny a truth (حقّاً) which you cannot distinguish from the falsehood of the one you are debating, and you only repel him from his falsehood by denying the truth (الحق). This is the forbidden (المحرّم) one, because you are like him: he denied a truth, and you denied another truth."

إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ ضَلَّ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ (Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are guided). Meaning: It is not incumbent upon you to guide them or to turn them away from error (الضّلالة); rather, upon you is only the conveyance (البلاغ). So, whoever has goodness in him, the evidence (البُرهان) and the advice (الوعظ) will suffice him; and whoever has no goodness in him, all efforts (الحيل) will fail with him, as if you are striking cold iron (حديد بارد) concerning him.

(١٢٦) وَإِنْ عاقَبْتُمْ فَعاقِبُوا بِمِثْلِ ما عُوقِبْتُمْ بِهِ وَلَئِنْ صَبَرْتُمْ لَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لِلصَّابِرِينَ. (126) If you punish, punish with the like of what you were punished with, but if you endure patiently, it is certainly better for the patient.

Al-Qummi (said): That was because the polytheists on the Day of Uhud mutilated the companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم who were martyred, among them Hamzah. So the Muslims said: "By Allah, if Allah grants us victory over them, we will certainly mutilate their best men." That is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَإِنْ عاقَبْتُمْ فَعاقِبُوا بِمِثْلِ ما عُوقِبْتُمْ بِهِ ** meaning, concerning the dead (بالأموات).

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He said on the Day of Uhud: "Who has knowledge of my uncle Hamzah?" Al-Harith ibn al-Samit said: "I know his location." So he came until he stood over Hamzah, but he disliked returning to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to inform Him. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "Search for your uncle, O Ali." So Ali عليه السلام came and stood over Hamzah, but he disliked returning to Him. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم came until He stood over him. When He saw what had been done to him, He wept, then He said: "I have never stood in a position more enraging to me than this place. If Allah enables me against Quraysh, I will certainly mutilate seventy men from them." Then Gabriel descended upon Him and said: **وَإِنْ عاقَبْتُمْ فَعاقِبُوا بِمِثْلِ ما عُوقِبْتُمْ بِهِ وَلَئِنْ صَبَرْتُمْ لَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لِلصَّابِرِينَ وَاصْبِرْ ** (If you punish, punish with the like of what you were punished with, but if you endure patiently, it is certainly better for the patient. And be patient.) So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Rather, I shall be patient."

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw what had been done to Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib, He said: "O Allah, all praise belongs to You, and to You is the complaint, and You are the Helper against what I see." Then He said: "If I prevail, I will certainly mutilate, and I will certainly mutilate." He (the Imam) said: So Allah revealed: **وَإِنْ عاقَبْتُمْ ** (If you punish) [the rest of] the Ayah. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I shall be patient, I shall be patient."

(١٢٧) وَاصْبِرْ وَما صَبْرُكَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ ** (127) And be patient, and your patience is only by Allah. except by His granting of success (توفيقِه) and His strengthening (تثبيته). وَلا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** and do not grieve for them, over your companions and what was done to them, for Allah has transferred them to the abode of His generosity (دار كرامته). وَلا تَكُ فِي ضَيْقٍ مِمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ ** and do not be distressed by what they scheme. in narrowness of chest (ضيق صدر) from their scheming (مكرهم). And it was read (وقرئ) with the kasrah (كسرِ) on the ḍād (الضّادِ).

(١٢٨) إِنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا ** (128) Indeed Allah is with those who are Godwary [meaning] polytheism (الشرك) and sins (المعاصي) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ مُحْسِنُونَ ** and those who are virtuous. [meaning] in their deeds (أعمالهم).

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثوابِ الاعمال) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat al-Nahl every month will be spared debt (المغرم) in this world and seventy types of afflictions (البلاء), the least of which are madness (الجنون), leprosy (الجذام), and vitiligo (البَرَص), and his dwelling will be in the Garden of Eden (جنّة عدْن), which is the center of the Gardens (الجنان). O Allah, grant us this by the right of Muhammad and his Household.
